title: Roast Chicken
story: The Sunday anchor. A good roast chicken is mostly about heat and patience: butter under the skin, a hot oven, and a proper rest so the juices settle back into the meat. Get those right and you barely need a recipe. Here's the one to learn by heart.

method.title: heat the oven
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Pat the chicken bone dry with kitchen paper, the drier the skin the crispier it roasts.

method.title: butter and season
method.body: Loosen the breast skin and push half the butter underneath. Rub the rest over the top, then season all over with salt.

method.title: stuff it
method.body: Push the lemon, garlic and thyme into the cavity. Tie the legs if you like a neat bird.

method.title: roast
method.body: Roast until the juices run clear and a thigh reads 75C. Baste once halfway through.

method.title: rest
method.body: Lift onto a board, cover loosely with foil and rest. This is not optional, it is the difference between juicy and dry.

method.title: carve
method.body: Carve the legs first, then the breast. Pour any resting juices back over the meat.

title: Marry Me Chicken
story: The internet's favourite weeknight showstopper, and for good reason. Seared chicken in a creamy sun-dried tomato and parmesan sauce that comes together in one pan. Rich, glossy and faintly addictive. Serve it to someone you like and see what happens.

method.title: sear
method.body: Season and lightly flour the chicken. Sear in the oil until deep golden on both sides, then lift out. It won't be cooked through yet.

method.title: build the base
method.body: In the same pan soften the garlic, sun-dried tomatoes and chilli flakes for a minute, scraping up the browned bits.

method.title: make it creamy
method.body: Pour in the stock and cream, stir in the parmesan and let it melt into a smooth, glossy sauce.

method.title: finish the chicken
method.body: Slide the chicken back in, spoon over the sauce and simmer gently until cooked through and the sauce coats a spoon.

method.title: serve
method.body: Tear over the basil. Serve with pasta or bread to mop the pan.

title: Chicken Parmesan
story: Crisp breaded chicken under a blanket of marinara and melted mozzarella. The trick is keeping the crumb crunchy: sauce only the top, grill it fast, and serve the moment the cheese blisters. A proper plate of comfort.

method.title: crumb the chicken
method.body: Set up flour, egg and breadcrumbs mixed with parmesan. Coat each cutlet in turn, pressing the crumb on firmly.

method.title: fry
method.body: Shallow fry in hot oil until deep golden and crisp on both sides. Drain on paper.

method.title: sauce the top
method.body: Spoon marinara over each cutlet, leaving the edges bare so they stay crunchy. Top with mozzarella.

method.title: grill
method.body: Slide under a hot grill until the cheese melts, bubbles and just catches colour.

method.title: serve
method.body: Rest a minute, then serve with spaghetti or a sharp green salad.

title: Honey Garlic Chicken
story: Sticky, sweet and savoury in under twenty minutes. Pan-fried chicken thighs tossed in a glaze of honey, garlic and soy that reduces to a lacquer. The kind of dinner that makes a Tuesday feel like a treat.

method.title: crisp the chicken
method.body: Toss the chicken in cornflour. Fry in hot oil, undisturbed at first, until golden and cooked through.

method.title: mix the glaze
method.body: Stir together the garlic, honey, soy, vinegar and chilli flakes.

method.title: glaze it
method.body: Pour the glaze into the pan and bubble hard, tossing, until it reduces and clings to every piece.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with sesame and spring onion. Serve over rice.

title: Chicken Fajitas
story: Sizzle is the whole point. Strips of spiced chicken and peppers seared hard and fast, then piled into warm tortillas with everything you fancy. Get the pan hot enough to char and it cooks in minutes.

method.title: spice the chicken
method.body: Toss the chicken with the spice mix, a little oil and salt. Leave to sit while you slice the veg.

method.title: sear the chicken
method.body: Get a pan smoking hot. Sear the chicken in a single layer until charred and cooked, then lift out.

method.title: char the veg
method.body: Add the peppers and onion to the same pan and cook hard until softened and blistered at the edges.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Return the chicken, squeeze over the lime and toss through.

method.title: build
method.body: Pile into warm tortillas with soured cream and salsa.

title: Fried Chicken
story: Buttermilk is the secret. An overnight soak tenderises the meat and helps the craggy crust cling. Fry it twice if you want it extra crisp, but even once gets you shatteringly good chicken. Worth the mess.

method.title: brine
method.body: Soak the chicken in the seasoned buttermilk, covered, in the fridge. Overnight is ideal.

method.title: dredge
method.body: Mix the flour with the spices. Lift each piece from the buttermilk and press firmly into the flour for a craggy crust.

method.title: heat the oil
method.body: Bring the oil to 170C. A pinch of flour should sizzle steadily, not violently.

method.title: fry
method.body: Fry in batches until deep golden and cooked through, turning now and then. Drain on a rack.

method.title: rest
method.body: Let it rest a few minutes so the crust sets and the juices settle.

title: Chicken Stir Fry
story: Fast food in the truest sense. The key is prep: chop everything before the pan goes on, because once it does you have about five minutes. High heat, quick toss, glossy sauce. Dinner before the rice is even cold.

method.title: prep everything
method.body: Slice the chicken and veg, mince the aromatics and stir the sauce together. Once you start cooking there is no time to chop.

method.title: sear the chicken
method.body: Get the wok smoking. Stir fry the chicken until just cooked, then push to the side or remove.

method.title: the veg
method.body: Add the garlic and ginger for a few seconds, then the veg, and toss over high heat until crisp-tender.

method.title: sauce it
method.body: Return the chicken, pour in the sauce and toss until it thickens and coats everything.

method.title: serve
method.body: Straight into bowls over rice or noodles.

title: Chicken Pot Pie
story: A pie to come home to. Tender chicken and vegetables in a creamy thyme gravy, capped with golden pastry. Make the filling ahead and it is barely any work to bake. Pure cold-weather comfort.

method.title: brown the chicken
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Brown the chicken in a little of the butter, then set aside.

method.title: soften the veg
method.body: Melt the rest of the butter, soften the onion and carrot, then stir in the flour and cook out for a minute.

method.title: make the gravy
method.body: Gradually whisk in the stock until smooth, then the cream and thyme. Simmer to a thick, silky sauce.

method.title: fill
method.body: Stir the chicken and peas back in, season well and tip into a pie dish.

method.title: top and bake
method.body: Lay over the pastry, trim, brush with egg and cut a steam hole. Bake until puffed and deep golden.

title: Lemon Chicken
story: Takeaway crispy lemon chicken, made brighter at home. Battered chicken fried until crunchy, then coated in a sharp, glossy lemon sauce. The sauce should be tangy enough to make you sit up, not cloying.

method.title: coat
method.body: Toss the chicken with egg, then cornflour, until each piece is dry and coated.

method.title: fry crisp
method.body: Fry in hot oil in batches until pale gold and crunchy. Drain on a rack.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Simmer the lemon juice, sugar and stock, then stir in the slaked cornflour until glossy and thickened.

method.title: coat and serve
method.body: Toss the crisp chicken through the warm sauce just before serving so it stays crunchy.

title: Chicken Shawarma
story: Street food spice in your own kitchen. Chicken thigh marinated in a warm blend of cumin, coriander and cinnamon, then roasted hard and sliced thin. Pile into flatbreads with garlic sauce and pickles and you've got the whole stall at home.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Mix the spice mix with the oil, lemon and garlic. Coat the chicken and chill, the longer the better.

method.title: roast hard
method.body: Heat the oven to 220C fan. Roast the chicken on a tray until charred at the edges and cooked through.

method.title: rest and slice
method.body: Rest briefly, then slice thin across the grain so it stays juicy.

method.title: build
method.body: Warm the flatbreads, spread with garlic yoghurt, pile in the chicken and add pickles.

title: BBQ Chicken
story: Sticky, smoky and best eaten with your hands. The move is to cook the chicken through first, then glaze in stages near the end so the sugars caramelise without burning. Grill or oven, the layering is what builds the lacquer.

method.title: start the chicken
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Rub the chicken with oil and salt and roast skin up until nearly cooked.

method.title: mix the glaze
method.body: Stir the bbq sauce with the honey and paprika.

method.title: glaze in layers
method.body: Brush with glaze and return to the oven. Repeat two or three times so it builds a sticky, caramelised coat.

method.title: rest
method.body: Rest a few minutes, then pile up and serve with extra sauce.

title: Buffalo Wings
story: The bar snack worth staying in for. Crisp baked or fried wings tossed in a glossy hot sauce and butter emulsion. The butter is what tames the vinegar bite and makes the sauce cling. Blue cheese on the side, non-negotiable.

method.title: dry and coat
method.body: Pat the wings very dry, then toss with the baking powder and salt. This is how you get crackly skin in the oven.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 220C fan. Bake on a rack, turning once, until deep golden and crisp.

method.title: melt the sauce
method.body: Warm the hot sauce, butter and honey together until smooth and glossy.

method.title: toss
method.body: Tip the hot wings into the sauce and toss until coated. Serve at once.

title: Chicken Enchiladas
story: A tray of warm comfort. Shredded spiced chicken rolled in tortillas, blanketed in red sauce and cheese, then baked until bubbling. Make the filling from leftover roast chicken and it comes together in no time.

method.title: make the filling
method.body: Heat the oven to 190C fan. Soften the onion and pepper, stir in the spice mix, then the chicken and beans.

method.title: roll
method.body: Spoon filling into each tortilla, roll up and lay seam down in a sauced baking dish.

method.title: sauce and cheese
method.body: Pour the rest of the sauce over and scatter with cheese.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake until the sauce bubbles at the edges and the cheese is melted and golden.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with coriander and serve with soured cream.

title: Teriyaki Chicken
story: Glossy, sweet-savoury and quick. Real teriyaki is just a reduction of soy, mirin and sugar brushed over chicken until it shines. Skip the bottled stuff, this takes five minutes and tastes worlds better.

method.title: sear
method.body: Fry the chicken skin side down in the oil until golden and crisp, then turn and cook through.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Pour off excess fat, add the soy, mirin, sugar and ginger and let it bubble to a syrup.

method.title: glaze
method.body: Turn the chicken in the sauce until lacquered and shining.

method.title: slice and serve
method.body: Slice and serve over rice, spooning the extra sauce on top.

title: Coronation Chicken
story: A retro classic that earns its place. Cold chicken in a lightly curried, creamy mango dressing with sultanas and almonds. Made for picnics, sandwiches and jacket potatoes. Use leftover roast chicken and it takes ten minutes.

method.title: bloom the spice
method.body: Warm the curry powder in a dry pan for a minute to wake it up, then let it cool.

method.title: mix the dressing
method.body: Stir the mayo, yoghurt, chutney and toasted curry powder into a smooth, golden dressing.

method.title: fold
method.body: Fold through the chicken, sultanas and most of the almonds. Taste and adjust.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter the last almonds over. Pile into sandwiches, jackets or onto leaves.

title: Chicken Kiev
story: The thrill is the cut: a crisp golden crust that gives way to a gush of garlic butter. Chill the butter hard and seal the seam well and you'll get that famous ooze instead of a leak. Worth the care.

method.title: make the butter
method.body: Mash the butter with garlic and parsley, roll into a log and chill until firm. Cold butter is everything here.

method.title: stuff
method.body: Cut a pocket in each breast, push in a piece of cold butter and seal the opening tightly.

method.title: crumb
method.body: Coat in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs. Double crumb the seam to stop leaks.

method.title: fry then bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 190C fan. Fry until golden, then bake on a rack until cooked through.

method.title: rest
method.body: Rest briefly, then cut in and let the garlic butter run.

title: Jerk Chicken
story: Big, fiery and aromatic. Jerk is all about the marinade: scotch bonnet, allspice, thyme and a little sweetness, worked deep into the meat and given time. Grill it for smoke, or roast hard for char. Either way, bring napkins.

method.title: blitz the marinade
method.body: Blend the marinade ingredients with the spice mix into a rough, fragrant paste.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Score the chicken, coat thoroughly and chill. Overnight gives the deepest flavour.

method.title: roast hard
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Roast until charred at the edges and cooked through, turning once.

method.title: rest
method.body: Rest a few minutes, then serve with lime to cut the heat.

title: Chicken Quesadilla
story: The five-minute fix. Spiced chicken and melting cheese pressed between tortillas until crisp and golden. Brilliant for leftovers, lunchboxes and late-night cravings. Cut into wedges and dunk in everything.

method.title: season
method.body: Toss the chicken with the paprika and cumin and a pinch of salt.

method.title: fill
method.body: Scatter cheese and chicken over half of each tortilla and fold over, or sandwich two.

method.title: crisp
method.body: Cook in a dry pan over medium heat, pressing down, until golden and the cheese melts. Flip once.

method.title: serve
method.body: Cut into wedges and serve with salsa and soured cream.

title: Chicken Satay
story: Skewers with a serious peanut sauce. Chicken marinated in turmeric and lemongrass, grilled till smoky, and served with a rich, savoury satay dip. The sauce is the star, make extra, you'll want it on everything.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Toss the chicken with turmeric, lemongrass, oil and salt. Chill while you make the sauce.

method.title: make the satay
method.body: Warm the peanut butter, coconut milk, soy, sugar and lime into a smooth, pourable sauce.

method.title: thread and grill
method.body: Thread onto skewers and grill or griddle over high heat until charred and cooked.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pile up with the warm satay sauce for dipping and a wedge of lime.

title: Nashville Hot Chicken
story: Fried chicken with a fierce streak. The crust is doused in a cayenne and brown sugar oil that glows red and builds slow heat. Traditionally served on white bread with pickles to cool the burn. Not for the faint-hearted.

method.title: brine
method.body: Soak the chicken in buttermilk in the fridge, overnight if you can.

method.title: dredge and fry
method.body: Coat in flour and fry at 170C until deep golden and cooked through. Drain on a rack.

method.title: make the hot oil
method.body: Whisk the cayenne, sugar and paprika into a ladle of the hot frying oil until smooth.

method.title: douse
method.body: Brush the fiery oil all over the crisp chicken so it soaks into every crag.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve on white bread with plenty of pickles to take the edge off.

title: Chicken Schnitzel
story: Thin, crisp and golden. The secret is pounding the chicken properly thin so it cooks in a flash, and frying in enough hot fat that the crumb puffs away from the meat. A squeeze of lemon is all it asks for.

method.title: pound thin
method.body: Place the chicken between sheets of cling film and pound to an even half centimetre. Thin is the whole trick.

method.title: crumb
method.body: Coat in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs, pressing on a light, even crust.

method.title: fry
method.body: Fry in shallow hot oil, swirling the pan so the crumb puffs, until golden on both sides.

method.title: serve
method.body: Drain briefly and serve at once with lemon to squeeze over.

title: Chicken Gyros
story: Greek street food, kitchen edition. Chicken marinated in oregano, garlic and lemon, roasted and sliced, then wrapped in warm pita with tzatziki, tomato and red onion. Bright, garlicky and built for eating on the move.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Coat the chicken in the oil, lemon, garlic and oregano. Chill to let the flavour soak in.

method.title: roast
method.body: Heat the oven to 220C fan. Roast until charred at the edges and cooked through.

method.title: rest and slice
method.body: Rest briefly, then slice thin.

method.title: wrap
method.body: Warm the pitas, spread with tzatziki, then pile in chicken, tomato and onion and roll.

title: Butter Chicken
story: Tikka's mellower cousin. The same charred, yoghurt-marinated chicken, but folded into a silky tomato and butter sauce softened with cream. Less spice, more comfort. The kasuri methi at the end is what makes it taste like the restaurant.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Coat the chicken in the yoghurt, ginger-garlic and garam masala and chill.

method.title: char
method.body: Sear the chicken hard in a hot pan until the edges catch, then set aside. It finishes in the sauce.

method.title: build the sauce
method.body: Soften the onion in the butter, add the passata and simmer until rich and split with the fat.

method.title: make it silky
method.body: Stir in the cream, sugar and crushed kasuri methi for that signature smooth, buttery finish.

method.title: combine
method.body: Return the chicken and simmer gently until cooked through and glossy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Swirl in a knob more butter and serve with naan.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
story: Forget the jar. Real tikka masala is two techniques stacked: chicken thigh marinated in spiced yoghurt until it's tender, then charred hard over flame so the edges blister and smoke, and only then folded into a tomato-butter gravy. Scroll on and we'll cook it together, step by step.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Whisk the yoghurt with the spice mix, ginger-garlic, lemon and salt. Fold through the chicken until every piece is coated, cover and rest in the fridge. Overnight is better, go watch a film.

method.title: heat up
method.body: Get a heavy pan or grill screaming hot. Lift the chicken from the marinade and shake off the excess so it chars instead of steams.

method.title: char it
method.body: Sear the pieces in batches over high heat. Don't move them, let the edges blister, blacken and catch. This char is the whole flavour of the dish.

method.title: build the gravy
method.body: Melt the ghee, soften the onion, stir in the tomato purée and cook it out. Add the passata and let it bubble and thicken into a deep, rich base.

method.title: cream it
method.body: Stir in the cream, sugar and crushed kasuri methi. Taste, adjust salt and heat. It should be glossy and balanced, not sharp.

method.title: combine
method.body: Fold the charred tikka back into the gravy with any resting juices. Loosen with a splash of water and simmer gently so the flavours marry.

method.title: finish & serve
method.body: Off the heat, scatter coriander and squeeze over lime. Serve with warm naan or basmati. You're done.

title: Chicken Curry
story: The everyday curry worth knowing by heart. An onion and tomato base, a handful of ground spices bloomed in oil, and chicken simmered until tender. No paste, no jar, just the building blocks that make every Indian curry sing.

method.title: soften the onion
method.body: Cook the onions in the oil slowly until deep golden. This is the flavour foundation, do not rush it.

method.title: bloom the spices
method.body: Stir in the ginger-garlic and ground spices and fry for a minute until fragrant.

method.title: make the masala
method.body: Add the tomatoes and cook down until thick and the oil separates at the edges.

method.title: simmer the chicken
method.body: Add the chicken and a splash of water, cover and simmer until tender.

method.title: finish
method.body: Stir through the garam masala and coriander, rest two minutes and serve.

title: Chicken Katsu Curry
story: Crisp panko chicken over a glossy, gently sweet curry sauce. The sauce is the soul: onions and carrots cooked down with curry powder until smooth and mellow. Wildly comforting, and easier than the queue at the canteen.

method.title: start the sauce
method.body: Soften the onion and carrot, stir in the curry powder and flour, then whisk in the stock. Simmer until thick.

method.title: blend
method.body: Blitz the sauce smooth, then stir in the soy and honey. Keep warm.

method.title: crumb the chicken
method.body: Coat each breast in flour, egg, then panko, pressing on a thick, even crust.

method.title: fry
method.body: Shallow fry in hot oil until deep golden and cooked through. Drain and slice.

method.title: serve
method.body: Lay the sliced katsu over rice and flood with the curry sauce.

title: Thai Green Curry
story: Fragrant, fresh and quick. Green curry paste fried in coconut cream until the oil splits, then loosened with the rest of the tin and brightened with fish sauce, lime and Thai basil. Hot, sweet, salty and sour, all at once.

method.title: split the cream
method.body: Simmer the thick top of the coconut milk with the paste until the oil splits and it smells fragrant.

method.title: sear the chicken
method.body: Add the chicken and stir to coat in the paste.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Pour in the rest of the coconut milk, add the aubergine and beans and simmer until tender.

method.title: balance
method.body: Season with fish sauce, sugar and lime until it tastes hot, sweet, salty and sour.

method.title: serve
method.body: Tear in the Thai basil and serve over jasmine rice.

title: Thai Red Curry
story: Deeper and warmer than the green. Red curry paste built on dried chillies, simmered with coconut milk and your choice of protein. Five minutes of paste-frying does most of the work, the rest is just a gentle bubble.

method.title: fry the paste
method.body: Simmer the paste in the thick coconut cream until split and aromatic.

method.title: add the chicken
method.body: Stir the chicken through the paste to coat.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the rest of the coconut milk, pepper and bamboo and simmer until cooked.

method.title: balance
method.body: Season with fish sauce, sugar and lime to taste.

method.title: serve
method.body: Spoon over rice with extra lime.

title: Beef Rendang
story: Patience made delicious. Beef simmered slowly in coconut milk and a fragrant spice paste until the liquid cooks away and the meat fries in its own rich, dark paste. It takes a couple of hours and rewards every minute.

method.title: make the paste
method.body: Blend the shallots, garlic, ginger, chillies and turmeric into a smooth paste.

method.title: fry the paste
method.body: Cook the paste in a little oil until fragrant and the rawness is gone.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the beef, coconut milk, lemongrass and lime leaves. Simmer gently, stirring now and then.

method.title: reduce and fry
method.body: Keep cooking as the liquid evaporates, until the beef fries in a thick, dark, oily paste.

method.title: serve
method.body: Rest briefly. Serve with steamed rice to soak up the spice.

title: Lamb Rogan Josh
story: A deep red, aromatic lamb curry built on yoghurt and kashmiri chilli rather than fierce heat. The colour is ceremonial, the flavour warm and fragrant with fennel and cardamom. Slow-cooked until the lamb gives way.

method.title: brown the lamb
method.body: Sear the lamb in the oil in batches until well browned, then set aside.

method.title: soften the onion
method.body: Cook the onions until deep golden, then add the ginger-garlic and whole spices.

method.title: bloom the chilli
method.body: Stir in the kashmiri chilli and fennel and fry for a minute to release the colour.

method.title: simmer slow
method.body: Return the lamb, whisk in the yoghurt off the heat, then add water and simmer gently until tender.

method.title: finish
method.body: Stir through the garam masala, rest and serve.

title: Chana Masala
story: Chickpeas with attitude. A tangy, spiced tomato and onion masala sharpened with amchur and a squeeze of lemon. Cheap, filling and entirely plant-based, this is the curry that proves you never needed the meat.

method.title: fry the onion
method.body: Cook the onions in the oil until golden, then add the ginger-garlic.

method.title: spice it
method.body: Stir in the ground spices and fry until fragrant.

method.title: make the masala
method.body: Add the tomatoes and cook to a thick paste, splashing in water if it sticks.

method.title: simmer the chickpeas
method.body: Add the chickpeas and a mugful of water, simmer until the sauce clings and thickens.

method.title: finish
method.body: Stir in the garam masala and a squeeze of lemon. Scatter coriander.

title: Saag Paneer
story: Soft cubes of paneer in a silky, spiced spinach sauce. The trick is cooking the spinach down hard and seasoning boldly so it never tastes flat. Golden-fried paneer adds the contrast that makes it crave-worthy.

method.title: fry the paneer
method.body: Brown the paneer cubes in the ghee until golden, then lift out.

method.title: wilt the spinach
method.body: Wilt the spinach in a splash of water, then blitz to a rough purée.

method.title: make the base
method.body: Soften the onion, add ginger-garlic, spices and tomatoes and cook to a thick masala.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Stir in the spinach and cream, then fold the paneer back through and warm.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with a little more garam masala and serve with naan.

title: Massaman Curry
story: The mild, nutty one. A Thai curry with Persian roots, warm with cinnamon and cardamom, rich with coconut and studded with potato and peanuts. Gentle heat, deep comfort, the curry for people who say they don't like spice.

method.title: fry the paste
method.body: Simmer the paste in the thick coconut cream until fragrant and split.

method.title: brown the meat
method.body: Add the meat and turn to coat in the paste.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Pour in the rest of the coconut milk with the potatoes and peanuts. Simmer until tender.

method.title: balance
method.body: Season with fish sauce, tamarind and sugar until rounded and mellow.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter extra peanuts and serve over rice.

title: Dal Tadka
story: Comfort in a bowl, and barely any effort. Yellow lentils simmered soft, then finished with a tadka: hot ghee crackling with cumin, garlic and chilli, poured over at the end. That sizzle is the whole dish.

method.title: cook the lentils
method.body: Simmer the lentils with the turmeric, tomato and water until soft and collapsing. Whisk to loosen.

method.title: season
method.body: Season the dal well with salt and keep it warm and loose.

method.title: make the tadka
method.body: Heat the ghee, crackle the cumin, then fry the garlic and chillies until golden. Kill the heat and stir in the kashmiri chilli.

method.title: pour over
method.body: Tip the sizzling tadka straight over the dal. Stir through and scatter coriander.

title: Prawn Curry
story: Quick, coastal and aromatic. Prawns cook in minutes, so the sauce does the heavy lifting: a coconut and tomato base scented with curry leaves and mustard seeds. Don't overcook the prawns, slip them in right at the end.

method.title: temper
method.body: Crackle the mustard seeds and curry leaves in the oil, then add the turmeric and chilli.

method.title: build the base
method.body: Soften the onion, add ginger-garlic and tomatoes and cook to a thick masala.

method.title: loosen
method.body: Pour in the coconut milk and simmer into a smooth, fragrant sauce.

method.title: add the prawns
method.body: Slip in the prawns and cook just until pink and curled. Do not overdo it.

method.title: serve
method.body: Squeeze over lime and scatter coriander.

title: Vegetable Korma
story: Mild, creamy and crowd-pleasing. Mixed vegetables in a gentle cashew and coconut sauce, warmly spiced but never hot. The cashews blended into the base are what make it luxuriously smooth. A gateway curry, in the best way.

method.title: soak the cashews
method.body: Soak the cashews in hot water while you start, then blend smooth with a splash of the soaking water.

method.title: fry the base
method.body: Soften the onion, add ginger-garlic and ground spices and fry until fragrant.

method.title: simmer the veg
method.body: Add the vegetables and a little water, cover and cook until just tender.

method.title: make it creamy
method.body: Stir in the cashew paste and coconut milk and warm through into a smooth sauce.

method.title: finish
method.body: Stir in the garam masala and serve with rice or naan.

title: Spaghetti Bolognese
story: Not authentic ragù, but the beloved British weeknight version, and proud of it. The secret is time: let the sauce blip away low and slow so the beef goes tender and the flavours deepen. A splash of milk keeps it mellow.

method.title: soffritto
method.body: Soften the onion, carrot and celery in oil until sweet, then add the garlic.

method.title: brown the beef
method.body: Turn up the heat and brown the mince well, breaking it up. Colour is flavour.

method.title: build the sauce
method.body: Stir in the purée, cook for a minute, then add the tomatoes and a mugful of water.

method.title: simmer low
method.body: Drop to a bare simmer, stir in the milk and let it reduce slowly until rich and thick.

method.title: toss and serve
method.body: Cook the spaghetti, drain and toss through the sauce with a little pasta water. Top with parmesan.

title: Spaghetti Carbonara
story: Four ingredients, zero cream, all technique. Eggs and pecorino whisked into a glossy sauce by the heat of the pasta alone, with crisp guanciale and a hit of black pepper. Work off the heat and it turns silky, not scrambled.

method.title: crisp the pork
method.body: Fry the guanciale slowly until the fat renders and it crisps. Keep the fat in the pan.

method.title: mix the sauce
method.body: Whisk the yolks, egg, pecorino and plenty of black pepper into a paste.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the spaghetti until al dente. Save a mug of the starchy water before draining.

method.title: emulsify off the heat
method.body: Off the heat, toss the hot pasta with the pork, then the egg mix, loosening with pasta water until glossy. Never let it boil.

method.title: serve
method.body: More pecorino, more pepper, straight to the table.

title: Chicken Alfredo
story: Indulgence on a fork. Fettuccine in a rich parmesan cream with seared chicken running through it. Keep the sauce loose with pasta water so it coats rather than claggs. Comfort food with no apologies.

method.title: sear the chicken
method.body: Season and sear the chicken in the oil until golden and cooked, then slice.

method.title: start the sauce
method.body: Melt the butter, soften the garlic, then pour in the cream and bring to a gentle simmer.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the fettuccine until al dente, saving a mug of pasta water.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Melt the parmesan into the cream, then toss through the pasta and chicken, loosening with pasta water.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with pepper and more parmesan.

title: Lasagna
story: The Sunday project that pays you back all week. Layers of slow ragù, creamy béchamel and pasta sheets, baked until the top is bronzed and the edges crisp. Make it ahead, it only improves overnight.

method.title: make the ragu
method.body: Brown the mince, soften the onion, add the tomatoes and purée and simmer low until rich.

method.title: make the bechamel
method.body: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, then whisk in the milk until thick and smooth. Stir in the parmesan.

method.title: layer
method.body: Layer ragù, sheets and béchamel in a dish, finishing with béchamel and mozzarella.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 190C fan. Bake until bubbling, bronzed and crisp at the edges.

method.title: rest
method.body: Rest before cutting so the layers hold. This is the hardest part.

title: Mac and Cheese
story: The ultimate cheese delivery system. A glossy roux-based sauce sharp with mature cheddar, baked under a crunchy crumb. Three cheeses if you're feeling it. Don't skip the mustard and a grate of nutmeg, they keep it from being one-note.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the macaroni a minute short of done, it finishes in the oven. Drain.

method.title: make the roux
method.body: Melt the butter, stir in the flour and cook out, then gradually whisk in the milk to a smooth sauce.

method.title: melt the cheese
method.body: Off the heat, stir in most of the cheddar, the mustard and nutmeg until glossy.

method.title: combine
method.body: Fold in the pasta, tip into a dish and top with the rest of the cheese and the crumbs.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Bake until golden and bubbling.

title: Pesto Pasta
story: Summer in a bowl, ready before the pasta water cools. Fresh basil pesto loosened with a little pasta water clings to every strand. Make the pesto by hand or blender, just keep it green and punchy. Ten minutes, no cooking the sauce.

method.title: make the pesto
method.body: Blitz or pound the basil, pine nuts, garlic and parmesan, then stream in the oil to a loose paste.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the pasta until al dente, saving a mug of pasta water.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss the drained pasta with the pesto off the heat, loosening with pasta water until it coats.

method.title: serve
method.body: Top with extra parmesan and a few whole basil leaves.

title: Penne Arrabbiata
story: Angry in the best way. A fiery tomato sauce built on garlic and dried chilli, ready in the time it takes the pasta to cook. Minimal ingredients, maximum punch. The trick is not to burn the garlic, gold not brown.

method.title: start the garlic
method.body: Warm the oil and gently fry the garlic and chilli flakes until the garlic turns pale gold.

method.title: add tomatoes
method.body: Tip in the tomatoes, season and simmer until thickened and glossy.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the penne until al dente, saving a little pasta water.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss the pasta through the sauce with a splash of pasta water.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with parsley. No cheese, traditionally, but no one's watching.

title: Cacio e Pepe
story: Roman magic from three things: pasta, pecorino and pepper. The sauce is an emulsion of cheese and starchy water, no cream in sight. It can be temperamental, so work fast and warm, and you'll be rewarded with pure, peppery silk.

method.title: toast the pepper
method.body: Toast the cracked pepper in a dry pan until fragrant.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the pasta in less water than usual so it gets extra starchy. Save plenty before draining.

method.title: make the cream
method.body: Mix the pecorino with a little warm pasta water into a smooth, loose paste. Not too hot or it clumps.

method.title: emulsify
method.body: Off the heat, toss the pasta with the pepper, then the cheese paste, adding water until glossy and clinging.

method.title: serve
method.body: Straight into warm bowls with more pepper.

title: Creamy Tuscan Pasta
story: The viral one, and worth the hype. A creamy parmesan sauce loaded with sun-dried tomatoes and wilted spinach. Rich, savoury and on the table in twenty minutes. Add chicken or keep it veggie, it works either way.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the pasta until al dente, saving a mug of water.

method.title: start the sauce
method.body: Soften the garlic and sun-dried tomatoes in a little of their oil.

method.title: make it creamy
method.body: Pour in the cream and melt in the parmesan to a glossy sauce.

method.title: wilt and toss
method.body: Stir in the spinach until wilted, then toss through the pasta with a splash of water.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with pepper and extra parmesan.

title: Baked Ziti
story: Lasagna's lazy, lovable cousin. Ziti tossed in tomato sauce with dollops of ricotta, blanketed in mozzarella and baked until molten. No layering, no fuss, just a big dish of bubbling comfort to feed a crowd.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Soften the onion and garlic, add the passata and oregano and simmer to a thick sauce.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the pasta a minute short of done and drain.

method.title: combine
method.body: Toss the pasta with the sauce, then fold through spoonfuls of ricotta.

method.title: top and bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Tip into a dish, top with mozzarella and parmesan and bake until golden.

method.title: serve
method.body: Rest a few minutes, scatter basil and dig in.

title: Pasta Puttanesca
story: Big, salty, savoury flavours from the storecupboard. Olives, capers, anchovy and chilli melted into tomato for a sauce with serious depth and almost no shopping. Punchy, fast and unapologetically bold.

method.title: melt the anchovy
method.body: Warm the oil and melt the anchovies with the garlic and chilli until they dissolve.

method.title: build the sauce
method.body: Add the olives, capers and tomatoes and simmer into a glossy, savoury sauce.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the spaghetti until al dente, saving a little water.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss the pasta through the sauce with a splash of pasta water.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with parsley. It needs no cheese.

title: Gnocchi Pomodoro
story: Pillowy potato dumplings in a simple, sweet tomato sauce. Shop-bought gnocchi cooks in two minutes, so a quick fresh sauce is all it needs. A little butter stirred in at the end makes it velvety. Weeknight bliss.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Soften the garlic in oil, add the passata, season and simmer until sweet and thick.

method.title: cook the gnocchi
method.body: Boil the gnocchi until they float, about two minutes, then drain.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Toss the gnocchi through the sauce, then stir in the butter for a glossy finish.

method.title: serve
method.body: Tear over basil and shower with parmesan.

title: Ravioli with Sage Butter
story: Restaurant elegance from one pan. Good shop-bought ravioli, dressed in nothing more than nutty browned butter, crisp sage and a squeeze of lemon. The butter is the sauce, so take it to that toasty, golden point.

method.title: cook the ravioli
method.body: Boil the ravioli gently until they float and are tender. Drain carefully.

method.title: brown the butter
method.body: Melt the butter with the sage and cook until the butter smells nutty and turns golden and the sage crisps.

method.title: dress
method.body: Add a squeeze of lemon and a splash of pasta water, then turn the ravioli through gently.

method.title: serve
method.body: Plate up, spoon over the butter and crisp sage and grate over parmesan.

title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
story: The midnight classic. Spaghetti, garlic, olive oil, chilli and parsley, that's the lot. The whole dish lives or dies on the garlic, sliced thin and coaxed to gold, and an emulsion of oil and pasta water that makes it glossy.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the spaghetti until al dente, saving a good mug of pasta water.

method.title: gild the garlic
method.body: Gently warm the oil with the garlic and chilli until the garlic is pale gold, never brown.

method.title: emulsify
method.body: Add a ladle of pasta water to the oil and swirl into a loose sauce.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss the pasta through with the parsley, adding water until it coats and shines.

method.title: serve
method.body: Straight to bowls. A little more oil on top.

title: Creamy Mushroom Pasta
story: Earthy, savoury and deeply comforting. Mushrooms fried hard until golden, then folded into a garlicky cream sauce with a flicker of thyme. Brown them properly in a hot pan, crowding steams them, space crisps them.

method.title: brown the mushrooms
method.body: Fry the mushrooms in a hot pan in batches until deep golden. Don't crowd them or they steam.

method.title: add aromatics
method.body: Stir in the garlic and thyme for a minute until fragrant.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the tagliatelle until al dente, saving a mug of water.

method.title: make it creamy
method.body: Pour in the cream, melt in the parmesan, then toss through the pasta with a splash of water.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with parsley and black pepper.

title: Sausage Rigatoni
story: A pub-favourite plate of pasta. Sausagemeat browned hard with fennel and chilli, simmered into tomato until rich, then clinging to ribbed rigatoni. Squeeze the meat from good sausages and you've basically made your own ragù in minutes.

method.title: brown the sausage
method.body: Fry the sausagemeat hard, breaking it up, with the fennel and chilli until deeply golden.

method.title: build the sauce
method.body: Add the garlic, then the tomatoes, and simmer until rich. Stir in the cream for body.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the rigatoni until al dente, saving a mug of water.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss the pasta through the sauce with a splash of water until it clings.

method.title: serve
method.body: Shower with parmesan and a little more chilli.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
story: The benchmark cookie: crisp edges, chewy middle, puddles of melting chocolate. Brown sugar keeps them soft, a short rest in the fridge deepens the flavour and stops them spreading thin. Pull them out looking slightly underbaked, they set as they cool.

method.title: cream
method.body: Beat the butter and both sugars until pale and fluffy, then beat in the egg and vanilla.

method.title: mix the dough
method.body: Fold in the flour and bicarb, then the chocolate, until just combined. Don't overwork it.

method.title: chill
method.body: Rest the dough in the fridge. This deepens the flavour and stops the cookies spreading flat.

method.title: scoop
method.body: Heat the oven to 180C fan. Scoop golf-ball mounds onto lined trays, leaving room to spread.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake until the edges are set but the middles still look soft. They firm up as they cool.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool on the tray for a few minutes before moving, or eat warm and gooey.

title: Fudgy Brownies
story: Dense, glossy and properly fudgy, never cakey. The shine on top comes from whisking the eggs and sugar hard until thick. Use good dark chocolate and pull them while the centre still has a wobble. Patience cooling is the only hard part.

method.title: melt
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Melt the chocolate and butter together until smooth, then cool slightly.

method.title: whisk for shine
method.body: Whisk the eggs and sugar until thick, pale and tripled in volume. This is what gives the shiny crust.

method.title: fold
method.body: Fold in the chocolate, then the flour and cocoa, gently, until just combined.

method.title: bake
method.body: Pour into a lined tin and bake until set at the edges but with a faint wobble in the centre.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool fully in the tin before cutting, the hardest step, for clean fudgy squares.

title: New York Cheesecake
story: Tall, dense and impossibly creamy. The New York way is all cream cheese, baked low and slow so it never cracks, then chilled overnight to set to that signature richness. A buttery biscuit base and nothing on top but confidence.

method.title: make the base
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Blitz the biscuits, mix with the butter and press firmly into a lined tin. Chill.

method.title: beat the filling
method.body: Beat the cream cheese and sugar smooth, then beat in the eggs, soured cream and vanilla. Don't overbeat or it cracks.

method.title: bake low
method.body: Pour over the base and bake until set at the edges with a gentle wobble in the middle.

method.title: cool slowly
method.body: Turn the oven off and leave the cheesecake inside with the door ajar to cool gradually.

method.title: chill
method.body: Chill in the fridge, overnight if you can. This is what sets the texture.

title: Tiramisu
story: No oven, no fuss, all elegance. Coffee-soaked sponge fingers layered with a cloud of sweetened mascarpone, dusted with bitter cocoa. The whole thing comes together in twenty minutes, then the fridge does the rest. Make it the day before.

method.title: make the cream
method.body: Whisk the yolks and sugar until pale, beat in the mascarpone, then fold in softly whipped cream.

method.title: dip
method.body: Briefly dip each sponge finger in the coffee, a quick soak, not a soggy one, and line a dish.

method.title: layer
method.body: Spread over half the cream, add another layer of dipped fingers, then the rest of the cream.

method.title: chill
method.body: Cover and chill until set. Overnight makes it even better.

method.title: finish
method.body: Dust thickly with cocoa just before serving.

title: Creme Brulee
story: The thrill is the crack. A silky vanilla custard set just barely, under a sheet of glassy caramelised sugar you shatter with a spoon. Bake it low in a water bath, chill it hard, and torch the top at the last second so it stays crisp.

method.title: infuse
method.body: Heat the oven to 140C fan. Warm the cream with the vanilla until steaming, then leave to infuse.

method.title: make the custard
method.body: Whisk the yolks and sugar, pour over the warm cream whisking, then strain.

method.title: bake in a bath
method.body: Pour into ramekins set in a tray of hot water. Bake until just set with a slight wobble.

method.title: chill
method.body: Cool, then chill until cold and firm.

method.title: brulee
method.body: Sprinkle each with sugar and torch or grill until the top is amber and glassy. Crack and eat.

title: Apple Pie
story: The pie that smells like home. Tart apples spiced with cinnamon, tucked under a golden, flaky lid. The secret to a crisp base and no soggy bottom is a hot oven and not drowning the filling in juice. A lattice if you're showing off.

method.title: make the pastry
method.body: Rub the butter into the flour and sugar, bring together with a little cold water, then chill.

method.title: spice the apples
method.body: Toss the apples with the sugar, cinnamon and cornflour to coat.

method.title: assemble
method.body: Line a tin with two-thirds of the pastry, pile in the apples, then lid with the rest. Seal and brush with egg.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 190C fan. Bake until deep golden and the filling bubbles through the steam hole.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool so the filling sets enough to slice. Serve warm.

title: Chocolate Mousse
story: Light as air, deeply chocolatey, and made from almost nothing. Good dark chocolate, eggs and a little cream, folded together with a gentle hand to keep all the bubbles. The fridge sets it to a perfect, spoonable cloud.

method.title: melt
method.body: Melt the chocolate gently and let it cool to just warm, then beat in the egg yolks.

method.title: whip the whites
method.body: Whisk the egg whites with the sugar to soft, glossy peaks.

method.title: whip the cream
method.body: Softly whip the cream to floppy peaks.

method.title: fold
method.body: Fold the cream into the chocolate, then the whites in two batches, keeping it airy.

method.title: chill
method.body: Spoon into glasses and chill until set.

title: Panna Cotta
story: Set cream at its most elegant. Just cream, sugar and vanilla, set with the barest amount of gelatine so it trembles when you tap it. The wobble is the whole point, set it too firm and you've made a different, sadder pudding.

method.title: soften the gelatine
method.body: Soak the gelatine leaves in cold water until floppy.

method.title: warm the cream
method.body: Heat the cream, milk, sugar and vanilla until steaming but not boiling.

method.title: set
method.body: Squeeze out the gelatine and stir into the warm cream until dissolved. Strain.

method.title: pour and chill
method.body: Pour into moulds or glasses and chill until just set with a wobble.

method.title: serve
method.body: Turn out, or serve in the glass, with fruit or a caramel drizzle.

title: Bread Pudding
story: Thrifty, spiced and gloriously old-fashioned. Stale bread soaked in a sweet, custardy mix with dried fruit and warm spice, then baked until set with crisp, sugary edges. Proof that the best puddings come from using things up.

method.title: soak
method.body: Pour the milk over the bread and leave to soak until soft, then mash a little.

method.title: mix
method.body: Beat in the eggs, sugar, melted butter, dried fruit and spices until combined.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Tip into a buttered dish, sprinkle with sugar and bake until set and golden.

method.title: serve
method.body: Cool slightly and serve warm, cut into thick squares.

title: Sticky Toffee Pudding
story: The crown jewel of British puddings. A soft date sponge drenched in a dark, buttery toffee sauce that soaks right in. Make extra sauce, you will want to pour it over everything. Best served warm with cold cream cutting through.

method.title: soak the dates
method.body: Pour boiling water over the dates with the bicarb and leave to soften, then mash.

method.title: make the batter
method.body: Cream the butter and sugar, beat in the eggs, then fold in the flour and the date mush.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Bake in a lined tin until risen and springy.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Melt the sugar, butter and cream together and simmer to a glossy toffee.

method.title: soak and serve
method.body: Poke holes in the warm sponge and pour over some sauce to soak in. Serve with the rest on the side.

title: Lemon Bars
story: Sharp, sunny and dangerously moreish. A crisp shortbread base under a set lemon curd that's all pucker and shine, finished with a snowfall of icing sugar. The contrast of buttery crumb and tart top is the whole joy.

method.title: make the base
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Rub the base ingredients to crumbs, press into a lined tin and bake until pale gold.

method.title: mix the lemon
method.body: Whisk the eggs, sugar, flour, lemon juice and zest into a smooth, sharp filling.

method.title: bake
method.body: Pour over the warm base and bake until just set with no wobble.

method.title: chill
method.body: Cool, then chill until firm enough to cut cleanly.

method.title: finish
method.body: Dust thickly with icing sugar and cut into squares.

title: Key Lime Pie
story: Florida sunshine in a tin. A buttery biscuit crust filled with a tangy lime and condensed milk custard that sets just by baking briefly. Five minutes of mixing, a quick bake, a good chill. Sharp, creamy and ridiculously easy.

method.title: make the base
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Blitz the biscuits, mix with butter, press into a tin and bake briefly.

method.title: mix the filling
method.body: Whisk the condensed milk, yolks, lime juice and zest until thick and smooth.

method.title: bake
method.body: Pour into the base and bake just until set with a slight wobble.

method.title: chill
method.body: Cool, then chill until firm.

method.title: serve
method.body: Top with whipped cream and extra lime zest.

title: Pecan Pie
story: The sweet, gooey centrepiece of any holiday table. A crisp pastry shell filled with toasted pecans set in a dark, buttery syrup. Rich enough that a thin slice does the job. Cold cream is not optional, it is balance.

method.title: blind bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Line a tin with pastry, fill with baking beans and blind bake until pale and set.

method.title: mix the filling
method.body: Whisk the syrup, sugar, butter, eggs and vanilla until smooth.

method.title: fill
method.body: Scatter the pecans into the shell and pour over the filling.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake until the filling is set with a slight wobble and the top is glossy.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool fully so the centre sets before slicing.

title: Churros
story: Fairground treats, made at home. A simple cooked dough piped into hot oil, fried until crisp and ridged, then rolled in cinnamon sugar. The thick chocolate dip is the law. Eat them hot, they wait for no one.

method.title: make the dough
method.body: Boil the water and butter, beat in the flour off the heat to a smooth paste, then beat in the egg.

method.title: heat the oil
method.body: Bring the oil to 180C. Pipe the dough through a star nozzle, snipping lengths straight into the oil.

method.title: fry
method.body: Fry in batches until deep golden and crisp, then drain.

method.title: roll
method.body: Toss the hot churros in the cinnamon sugar while still warm.

method.title: dip
method.body: Melt the chocolate with a splash of cream and serve for dunking.

title: Banoffee Pie
story: A no-bake crowd-pleaser built on banana and toffee. Biscuit base, a thick layer of caramel, sliced banana and clouds of cream, finished with chocolate. Outrageously easy, outrageously good. The only skill is restraint.

method.title: make the base
method.body: Blitz the biscuits, mix with the butter and press into a tin. Chill until firm.

method.title: caramel
method.body: Spread the caramel evenly over the chilled base.

method.title: banana
method.body: Layer the sliced banana over the caramel.

method.title: cream
method.body: Pile the whipped cream on top and swirl.

method.title: finish
method.body: Grate over the chocolate and chill until ready to serve.

title: Dubai Chocolate Bar
story: The viral sensation worth the hype. A thick chocolate shell filled with crunchy toasted kataifi pastry folded through pistachio cream. Snap into it for that famous crackle. A little fiddly, hugely impressive, and surprisingly doable at home.

method.title: toast the kataifi
method.body: Fry the shredded kataifi in the butter, stirring, until deep golden and crisp. Cool.

method.title: line the mould
method.body: Melt the chocolate and brush a thick layer into a bar mould or lined tin. Chill until set.

method.title: mix the filling
method.body: Stir the cooled kataifi through the pistachio cream and tahini until coated and crunchy.

method.title: fill and seal
method.body: Press the filling into the shell, then cover with the rest of the melted chocolate.

method.title: set
method.body: Chill until fully set, then turn out and snap into pieces.

title: Victoria Sponge
story: The cake the whole country measures others against. Two feather-light sponges sandwiched with jam and cream, dusted with sugar. The classic ratio of equal eggs, butter, sugar and flour never fails. A cake that needs no occasion.

method.title: cream
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy.

method.title: combine
method.body: Beat in the eggs and vanilla, then fold in the flour to a smooth batter.

method.title: bake
method.body: Divide between two lined tins and bake until risen, golden and springy.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool completely on a rack before filling.

method.title: fill
method.body: Spread one sponge with jam, the other with cream, sandwich and dust with sugar.

title: Chocolate Cake
story: The one you bake for birthdays and bad days alike. A deep, moist crumb made tender by a splash of hot coffee in the batter, layered with silky chocolate buttercream. Rich without being heavy. Everyone needs a chocolate cake they trust.

method.title: mix the dry
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Whisk the flour, sugar, cocoa and bicarb together.

method.title: add the wet
method.body: Whisk in the eggs, buttermilk and oil, then the hot coffee, to a loose batter. The coffee deepens the chocolate.

method.title: bake
method.body: Divide between two lined tins and bake until a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: make the buttercream
method.body: Beat the butter, icing sugar and cocoa until smooth and fluffy.

method.title: assemble
method.body: Cool the cakes fully, then sandwich and cover with the buttercream.

title: Carrot Cake
story: Spiced, moist and topped with tangy cream cheese frosting. Grated carrot keeps it incredibly tender and the warm spices do the heavy lifting. Walnuts for crunch, if you like. A cake that somehow feels almost virtuous.

method.title: mix the batter
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Whisk the oil, sugar and eggs, then fold in the flour, spices, carrot and walnuts.

method.title: bake
method.body: Divide between two lined tins and bake until a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool the cakes completely before frosting.

method.title: make the frosting
method.body: Beat the cream cheese, butter and icing sugar until smooth, not runny.

method.title: frost
method.body: Sandwich and cover with frosting, then scatter with extra walnuts.

title: Red Velvet Cake
story: Striking to look at, gentle to eat. A soft, lightly cocoa-ed sponge with a dramatic red crumb and a tangy buttermilk softness, under glossy cream cheese frosting. More about texture and tang than chocolate. A showstopper that's secretly simple.

method.title: mix the dry
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Whisk the flour, sugar and cocoa.

method.title: add the wet
method.body: Whisk in the eggs, buttermilk, oil, colouring and vinegar to a smooth, vivid batter.

method.title: bake
method.body: Divide between two lined tins and bake until springy and clean to a skewer.

method.title: frost
method.body: Beat the frosting smooth, then sandwich and cover the cooled cakes.

method.title: finish
method.body: Smooth the top and chill briefly to set before slicing.

title: Lemon Drizzle Cake
story: The teatime hero. A buttery loaf soaked while warm with a sharp lemon syrup that crackles into a sugary crust on top. The drizzle going on hot is the whole trick, it sinks in and keeps the crumb moist for days. If it lasts that long.

method.title: cream
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Beat the butter and sugar pale, beat in the eggs, then fold in the flour and zest.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake in a lined loaf tin until risen and a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: make the drizzle
method.body: Stir the lemon juice and sugar together, leaving the sugar grainy.

method.title: soak
method.body: Prick the warm cake all over and spoon the drizzle on so it soaks in and crusts on top.

title: Vanilla Cupcakes
story: Light, vanilla-scented sponges crowned with swirls of buttercream. The base everyone should know, ready to decorate however the day demands. Don't overfill the cases, two-thirds is the sweet spot for a perfect domed top.

method.title: make the batter
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Beat the butter and sugar, add the eggs and vanilla, then fold in the flour.

method.title: bake
method.body: Fill the cases two-thirds full and bake until risen and springy.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool completely before icing or the buttercream slides off.

method.title: make the buttercream
method.body: Beat the butter, icing sugar and vanilla until pale and fluffy.

method.title: decorate
method.body: Pipe or swirl onto the cupcakes and finish with sprinkles.

title: Coffee Cake
story: Coffee and walnut, the timeless duo. A tender sponge flavoured with real espresso, layered and topped with smooth coffee buttercream and toasted walnuts. Grown-up, gentle and made for a slow afternoon. The kind of cake a nan would approve of.

method.title: make the batter
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Cream the butter and sugar, add eggs, then fold in flour, espresso and walnuts.

method.title: bake
method.body: Divide between two lined tins and bake until springy.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool fully before icing.

method.title: buttercream
method.body: Beat the butter, icing sugar and espresso until smooth and coffee-coloured.

method.title: assemble
method.body: Sandwich and top with the buttercream and walnut halves.

title: Pound Cake
story: The recipe you can remember without a book: a pound each of butter, sugar, eggs and flour. Dense, buttery and plain in the best sense, perfect sliced and toasted or piled with fruit and cream. Old-fashioned and entirely reliable.

method.title: cream
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Beat the butter and sugar until very pale and fluffy, a few minutes longer than feels necessary.

method.title: add eggs
method.body: Beat in the eggs one at a time, then the vanilla.

method.title: fold
method.body: Fold in the flour and baking powder gently.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake in a lined loaf tin until deep golden and a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool in the tin, then slice. Lovely plain or toasted with butter.

title: Funfetti Cake
story: Pure joy in cake form. A soft vanilla sponge shot through with rainbow sprinkles, under a fluffy vanilla buttercream and yet more sprinkles. Designed to make people grin. Use proper jimmies, not nonpareils, so the colours don't bleed.

method.title: make the batter
method.body: Heat the oven to 170C fan. Cream the butter and sugar, add the eggs and vanilla, fold in the flour, then the sprinkles last.

method.title: bake
method.body: Divide between two lined tins and bake until springy.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool completely before frosting.

method.title: buttercream
method.body: Beat the butter, icing sugar and vanilla until fluffy.

method.title: decorate
method.body: Sandwich and cover with buttercream, then shower with more sprinkles.

title: Black Forest Cake
story: A retro classic that deserves a comeback. Layers of chocolate sponge soaked in kirsch, piled with cherries and whipped cream, finished with chocolate curls. Indulgent and a little theatrical. The cherry-and-chocolate pairing never goes out of style.

method.title: make the sponge
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Whisk the dry ingredients, then the wet, into a batter and bake in two lined tins.

method.title: cool and soak
method.body: Cool the sponges, then brush with a little cherry syrup and kirsch.

method.title: layer
method.body: Sandwich with whipped cream and cherries, keeping some cherries for the top.

method.title: cover
method.body: Coat the cake in cream and press shaved chocolate around the sides.

method.title: finish
method.body: Top with the reserved cherries and more chocolate curls.

title: Angel Food Cake
story: The lightest cake there is, made from whipped egg whites and not a scrap of fat. Cloud-like, faintly chewy and barely sweet, it begs for fruit and cream. Cool it upside down so it keeps its airy height. A clean, dramatic bake.

method.title: whip the whites
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Whisk the whites with the cream of tartar to soft peaks, then add half the sugar a spoon at a time to glossy, firm peaks.

method.title: fold
method.body: Sift the flour with the rest of the sugar and fold in gently in batches with the vanilla, keeping the air.

method.title: bake
method.body: Spoon into an ungreased tube tin and bake until risen and golden and springy.

method.title: cool upside down
method.body: Invert the tin and leave to cool completely so the cake holds its height.

method.title: serve
method.body: Release, then serve in clouds with berries and cream.

title: Molten Lava Cake
story: The dinner-party trick with a gooey heart. Individual chocolate sponges baked just long enough to set the outside while the centre stays liquid. Timing is everything, a minute too long and the magic is gone. Have the spoons ready.

method.title: melt
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Melt the chocolate and butter together, then cool slightly.

method.title: mix
method.body: Whisk the eggs and sugar until pale, fold in the chocolate, then the flour.

method.title: fill
method.body: Butter and cocoa four ramekins and divide the batter between them.

method.title: bake short
method.body: Bake until the tops are set but the centres still wobble. This timing is the whole game.

method.title: turn out
method.body: Rest a minute, turn out and serve at once so the centre runs.

title: Banana Bread
story: The reason to let bananas go black. The riper they are, the sweeter and more fragrant the loaf. One bowl, no mixer needed, and forgiving enough to make half asleep. Toast leftover slices with butter and you'll never waste a banana again.

method.title: mash
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Mash the bananas in a big bowl until almost smooth.

method.title: mix the wet
method.body: Stir in the melted butter, sugar, eggs and vanilla.

method.title: fold
method.body: Fold in the flour until just combined. Lumps are fine, overmixing makes it tough.

method.title: bake
method.body: Pour into a lined loaf tin and bake until deep golden and a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool in the tin, then slice. Excellent toasted with butter.

title: Scones
story: Light, tall and ready for jam and cream. The secret is a gentle hand, work the dough as little as possible and don't twist the cutter, or they bake lopsided. Eat them the day you make them, warm, with the cream-or-jam-first debate optional.

method.title: rub in
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Rub the butter into the flour and sugar until like fine crumbs.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Stir in the milk to a soft dough, handling it as little as you can.

method.title: cut
method.body: Pat out to thick, stamp out rounds with a straight push, no twisting.

method.title: bake
method.body: Brush with egg and bake until well risen and golden.

method.title: serve
method.body: Cool slightly and serve warm with jam and clotted cream.

title: Blueberry Muffins
story: Bakery-style muffins with sky-high domed tops. The tricks are a hot start to lift the dome, a thick batter, and not crushing the blueberries when you fold them in. Coarse sugar on top for that crunchy bakery finish.

method.title: mix the dry
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Whisk the flour, sugar and baking powder.

method.title: add the wet
method.body: Whisk the eggs, oil and milk, then fold into the dry to a thick batter. Don't overmix.

method.title: fold the berries
method.body: Gently fold in the blueberries so they stay whole.

method.title: bake hot
method.body: Fill the cases high, sprinkle with sugar, and bake. The hot oven gives that domed top.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool a few minutes in the tin, then enjoy warm.

title: Cinnamon Rolls
story: Soft, swirled and smothered in icing. The reward for a little patience with an enriched dough that proves to a pillowy cloud, rolled around a cinnamon-sugar butter. Pull them apart warm while the icing melts into the cracks. Worth every minute.

method.title: make the dough
method.body: Mix and knead all the dough ingredients to a smooth, soft, elastic dough.

method.title: first prove
method.body: Cover and leave somewhere warm until doubled in size.

method.title: fill and roll
method.body: Roll out, spread with butter, scatter the cinnamon sugar, roll up and slice into nine.

method.title: second prove and bake
method.body: Prove in a tin until puffy, then bake at 180C fan until golden.

method.title: ice
method.body: Mix the icing sugar with a splash of milk and drizzle over while warm.

title: Shortbread
story: Three ingredients, infinite charm. Butter, sugar and flour worked just enough to hold together, baked low and slow to a pale gold with a sandy snap. The high proportion of butter is the whole story, so use a good one.

method.title: cream
method.body: Beat the butter and sugar until just combined, not fluffy, you want a short, sandy texture.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Work in the flour and salt until it clumps into a dough.

method.title: shape
method.body: Press into a tin or roll and cut into fingers. Prick all over with a fork.

method.title: bake low
method.body: Heat the oven to 150C fan. Bake until pale gold, no more, the colour should stay light.

method.title: finish
method.body: Dust with sugar and cut while warm, then cool to crisp.

title: Flapjacks
story: Chewy, buttery oat bars and the easiest bake going. Melt, mix, press, bake. The ratio of butter, sugar and syrup is what keeps them chewy not crumbly, and cutting while warm stops them shattering. A lunchbox legend.

method.title: melt
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Melt the butter, sugar and syrup together until smooth.

method.title: mix
method.body: Stir in the oats until evenly coated.

method.title: press
method.body: Press firmly into a lined tin, the firmer you press the chewier they set.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake until golden at the edges but still soft in the middle.

method.title: cut warm
method.body: Cut into bars while warm, then let them cool and firm up in the tin.

title: Croissants
story: The everest of home baking, and a brilliant thing to conquer. Layering cold butter into dough and folding it again and again builds hundreds of paper-thin layers that puff into shatteringly flaky crescents. It takes patience and a cold kitchen, but the smell alone is the reward.

method.title: make the dough
method.body: Mix the dough ingredients to a smooth dough, then chill it for an hour to relax.

method.title: laminate
method.body: Encase the butter block in the dough, then roll and fold into thirds three times, chilling well between each fold.

method.title: shape
method.body: Roll out thin, cut into triangles and roll up into crescents.

method.title: prove
method.body: Prove somewhere cool until visibly puffy and jiggly.

method.title: bake
method.body: Glaze with egg and bake at 200C fan until deep golden and flaky.

title: Chocolate Chip Muffins
story: Tall, tender and studded with chocolate. The muffin method keeps them light: mix wet and dry separately and bring them together with a light hand. A hot oven start gives the bakery dome, and a few extra chips on top never hurt.

method.title: mix the dry
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Whisk the flour, sugar and baking powder.

method.title: add the wet
method.body: Whisk the eggs, oil and milk, then fold into the dry until just combined.

method.title: fold the chips
method.body: Fold in most of the chocolate chips.

method.title: bake hot
method.body: Fill the cases high, top with extra chips and bake until domed and set.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool briefly and eat warm with the chocolate still soft.

title: Gingerbread
story: Dark, sticky and deeply spiced. A treacly loaf that's almost better a day or two on, when the spice settles and the crumb turns fudgy. Ginger, cinnamon and mixed spice do the warming, treacle does the depth. Pure cosy.

method.title: melt
method.body: Heat the oven to 160C fan. Gently melt the butter, sugar, treacle and syrup together, then cool a little.

method.title: mix
method.body: Whisk the flour and spices, then stir in the melted mix, the egg and the milk with the bicarb to a loose batter.

method.title: bake
method.body: Pour into a lined loaf tin and bake until risen and a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: rest
method.body: Cool fully, then wrap and leave a day if you can, it turns wonderfully sticky.

title: Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
story: Chewy, spiced and quietly addictive. Oats give body and chew, brown sugar keeps them soft, and a little cinnamon ties it together. Plump the raisins in hot water first so they stay juicy rather than turning to hard little stones.

method.title: plump the raisins
method.body: Soak the raisins in hot water while you start, then drain. This keeps them soft.

method.title: cream
method.body: Beat the butter and sugar, then beat in the egg.

method.title: mix
method.body: Fold in the flour, oats, cinnamon and raisins to a stiff dough.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 180C fan. Scoop onto trays and bake until golden at the edges.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool on the tray a few minutes to set, then enjoy chewy.

title: Snickerdoodles
story: Soft, tangy and rolled in cinnamon sugar. Cream of tartar is the secret, giving that signature slight tang and chewy crackle. They spread into crinkled, sugar-crusted rounds. Simple, nostalgic and impossible to eat just one.

method.title: cream
method.body: Beat the butter and sugar pale, then beat in the egg.

method.title: mix
method.body: Fold in the flour, cream of tartar and bicarb to a soft dough.

method.title: roll
method.body: Roll into balls and toss in the cinnamon sugar to coat.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 180C fan. Bake until puffed and crackled but still soft.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool on the tray to set the chewy middle.

title: Peanut Butter Cookies
story: Nutty, soft and marked with the classic fork criss-cross. The crosshatch isn't just for looks, it flattens the dense dough so it bakes evenly. Use a good crunchy peanut butter for texture. Three-bowl easy, lunchbox approved.

method.title: mix
method.body: Beat the peanut butter, sugar and egg until smooth, then fold in the flour and bicarb.

method.title: shape
method.body: Roll into balls, place on trays and flatten with a fork in a criss-cross to help them bake evenly.

method.title: bake
method.body: Heat the oven to 180C fan. Bake until just set at the edges but still soft.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool on the tray to firm up, then enjoy soft and chewy.

title: Sourdough Bread
story: A loaf with a soul. Just flour, water and salt, leavened slowly by a living starter into bread with an open, chewy crumb and a blistered, crackling crust. It takes time and a little attention, but the rhythm of folds and the smell from the oven are the reward.

method.title: mix and rest
method.body: Mix the flour, water and starter to a shaggy dough and rest, then work in the salt.

method.title: stretch and fold
method.body: Over a few hours, give the dough a set of stretches and folds every half hour as it strengthens.

method.title: shape
method.body: Shape into a tight round, then place seam-up in a floured banneton.

method.title: cold prove
method.body: Prove in the fridge overnight to develop flavour and structure.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake in a screaming-hot covered pot, then uncovered, until deep brown and hollow-sounding.

title: Focaccia
story: Dimpled, golden and drenched in good olive oil. A wet, easy dough that needs no kneading skill, just time, then a generous pour of oil and a confident press of the fingers. Crisp and salty on top, pillowy within. Possibly the most forgiving bread there is.

method.title: mix
method.body: Stir the flour, water, yeast and salt to a wet, sticky dough, no kneading needed.

method.title: rise
method.body: Cover and leave until doubled and bubbly.

method.title: oil and prove
method.body: Tip into a well-oiled tray, spread out and prove again until puffy.

method.title: dimple
method.body: Drench in olive oil and press your fingers all over to make deep dimples.

method.title: bake
method.body: Scatter with flaky salt and rosemary and bake at 220C fan until golden and crisp.

title: Naan
story: Soft, blistered and made for scooping up curry. Yoghurt in the dough keeps it tender and gives a gentle tang. Cooked fast in a screaming-hot pan to char and puff, then brushed with garlic butter. Once you've made fresh naan, the supermarket sort won't do.

method.title: make the dough
method.body: Mix the flour, yoghurt, yeast, sugar, water and a little salt to a soft dough and knead until smooth.

method.title: rise
method.body: Cover and leave until doubled in size.

method.title: shape
method.body: Divide and roll into teardrops, not too thin.

method.title: cook
method.body: Cook in a dry, very hot pan until it puffs and chars in spots, then flip.

method.title: brush
method.body: Brush hot with the garlic butter and scatter with coriander.

title: Garlic Bread
story: The side that always disappears first. A baguette split and slathered with garlic-and-herb butter, then baked until the edges crisp and the middle goes soft and buttery. Cheap, fast and dangerously good. Make extra, because someone always wants more.

method.title: make the butter
method.body: Beat the soft butter with the garlic, parsley and a pinch of salt.

method.title: split
method.body: Slice the bread most of the way through at intervals, or split lengthways.

method.title: slather
method.body: Push the garlic butter into every cut and over the cut faces.

method.title: bake
method.body: Wrap in foil and bake at 200C fan, opening for the last few minutes to crisp.

title: Dinner Rolls
story: Soft, fluffy and pull-apart warm from the oven. An enriched dough with a little butter and milk gives a tender, cloud-like crumb. Baked snug together so they rise tall and tear apart in soft pillows. The kind of thing that makes a simple dinner feel special.

method.title: make the dough
method.body: Mix everything with a little salt and knead until smooth, soft and elastic.

method.title: first rise
method.body: Cover and leave until doubled in size.

method.title: shape
method.body: Knock back, divide and roll into tight balls, then sit them snug in a tin.

method.title: prove
method.body: Cover and prove again until puffy and touching.

method.title: bake
method.body: Brush with egg and bake at 180C fan until golden, then brush with butter.

title: Soda Bread
story: Bread in an hour, no yeast and no waiting. Bicarbonate of soda and buttermilk do the lifting, so it's mixed and baked while the kettle's barely boiled. A dense, nubbly loaf with a craggy crust, best eaten warm the day it's made, thickly buttered.

method.title: mix dry
method.body: Stir the flours, bicarb and salt together in a big bowl.

method.title: bring together
method.body: Pour in the buttermilk and mix quickly to a soft, sticky dough, don't overwork it.

method.title: shape
method.body: Shape into a round on a floured tray and cut a deep cross in the top.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 200C fan until risen, browned and hollow when tapped underneath.

method.title: cool
method.body: Cool a little on a rack, then eat warm with lots of butter.

title: Ciabatta
story: That slipper-shaped loaf with the wild, holey crumb. A very wet dough and a long, slow ferment build the big open holes and chewy texture. Handling it is messy and a little nerve-wracking, but the flour-dusted, crackling result is bakery-worthy.

method.title: mix wet dough
