method.body: Mix to a very wet, sticky dough, this high hydration is what makes the holes.

method.title: fold
method.body: Give it gentle folds in the bowl every half hour as it ferments and strengthens.

method.title: long rise
method.body: Leave until tripled, bubbly and alive.

method.title: shape gently
method.body: Tip onto a heavily floured surface and coax into two slipper shapes without knocking out the air.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 230C fan with steam until deep golden and crackling.

title: Brioche
story: Bread that behaves like cake. Enriched with eggs and a serious amount of butter, brioche bakes up golden, tender and faintly sweet, with a soft, pull-apart crumb. It takes patience to work all that butter in, but a warm slice with jam is pure luxury.

method.title: make the dough
method.body: Mix the flour, eggs, yeast, sugar and salt and knead to a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: add the butter
method.body: Work in the soft butter a little at a time until silky and glossy, this is the test of patience.

method.title: chill
method.body: Rest in the fridge so the butter firms and the dough is workable.

method.title: shape and prove
method.body: Shape into a tin or rolls and prove until puffy.

method.title: bake
method.body: Glaze with egg and bake at 170C fan until deep golden and glossy.

title: Chicken Noodle Soup
story: The bowl you reach for when you're under the weather, or just want to feel looked after. A clear, golden broth with sweet soft vegetables, shredded chicken and slippery noodles. Simple, restorative and quietly perfect.

method.title: soften the veg
method.body: Sweat the onion, carrot and celery in a little oil until softened and sweet.

method.title: poach the chicken
method.body: Add the stock and chicken, bring to a gentle simmer and cook until the chicken is done.

method.title: shred
method.body: Lift out the chicken, shred with two forks and return it to the pot.

method.title: add noodles
method.body: Drop in the noodles and simmer until tender.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season well, stir through the parsley and ladle into bowls.

title: Tomato Soup
story: Better than any tin, and barely more effort. Roasting the tomatoes first concentrates their sweetness and adds a little smoky depth. Blend until silky, swirl in a little cream, and serve with the only correct partner: a cheese toastie.

method.title: roast
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Roast the tomatoes, onion and garlic in the oil until soft and caramelised.

method.title: blend
method.body: Tip into a pan with the stock and basil and blitz until silky smooth.

method.title: warm
method.body: Bring to a gentle simmer, then stir in the cream and season well.

method.title: serve
method.body: Ladle into bowls with a swirl of cream and more basil.

title: Minestrone
story: A whole garden in a pot. This hearty vegetable and bean soup is endlessly forgiving, use whatever veg needs eating, plus pasta for body and a parmesan rind in the broth for depth. A bowl that's somehow light and filling at once.

method.title: soffritto
method.body: Soften the onion, carrot and celery in oil until sweet, the base of any good minestrone.

method.title: build
method.body: Add the courgette, tomatoes, beans and stock and bring to a simmer.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Simmer until the vegetables are tender and the broth is rich.

method.title: add pasta
method.body: Stir in the pasta and cook until al dente, adding water if it thickens too much.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season well and serve with parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil.

title: French Onion Soup
story: Patience turned to gold. The whole dish rests on cooking onions low and slow until they're deeply caramelised and sweet, then simmering them in good beef stock. Crowned with a cheese-laden crouton grilled until bubbling. Bistro comfort at its finest.

method.title: caramelise
method.body: Cook the onions in the butter with the sugar over low heat, stirring often, until deeply golden and jammy. This is the whole dish, don't rush it.

method.title: deglaze
method.body: Pour in the wine and scrape up the sticky brown bits.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the stock and simmer until rich and savoury. Season.

method.title: top
method.body: Ladle into ovenproof bowls, float a slice of baguette on each and pile with gruyere.

method.title: grill
method.body: Grill until the cheese is molten, bubbling and golden.

title: Butternut Squash Soup
story: Velvety, golden and gently sweet. Roasting the squash deepens the flavour and makes it blend like a dream. A little chilli and ginger keep it from being one-note. The kind of soup that makes a grey day feel manageable.

method.title: roast
method.body: Heat the oven to 200C fan. Roast the squash and onion in the oil with the chilli until soft and caramelised.

method.title: blend
method.body: Tip into a pan with the ginger and stock and blitz until completely smooth.

method.title: warm
method.body: Bring to a gentle simmer and season well, loosening with water if needed.

method.title: serve
method.body: Ladle out with a swirl of cream or oil and some toasted seeds.

title: Chicken Tortilla Soup
story: A spiced, brothy bowl with all the fun on top. A smoky tomato and chilli base with shredded chicken, finished with crunchy tortilla strips, avocado, lime and cheese. The toppings are half the joy, so don't hold back.

method.title: build the base
method.body: Soften the onion and garlic, then stir in the spice mix until fragrant.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the tomatoes, stock and chicken and simmer until the chicken is cooked.

method.title: crisp the tortillas
method.body: Meanwhile fry or bake the tortilla strips until crisp and golden.

method.title: shred
method.body: Lift out the chicken, shred and return to the soup. Season with lime.

method.title: serve
method.body: Top each bowl with tortilla strips, avocado, cheese and coriander.

title: Ramen
story: A whole bowl of comfort, fast-tracked for a weeknight. A savoury soy and ginger broth, springy noodles, a jammy soft egg and whatever toppings you fancy. Not a three-day tonkotsu, but deeply satisfying in thirty minutes.

method.title: build the broth
method.body: Simmer the stock with the soy, miso, ginger and garlic to infuse, then strain.

method.title: jammy eggs
method.body: Soft boil the eggs for six and a half minutes, cool in iced water and peel.

method.title: cook the noodles
method.body: Cook the noodles to package time and divide between bowls.

method.title: assemble
method.body: Wilt the pak choi in the broth, then ladle over the noodles.

method.title: top
method.body: Halve the eggs on top and finish with spring onion and chilli oil.

title: Pho
story: Fragrant, clear and deeply soothing. The magic is in charring the aromatics and toasting whole spices so the beef broth tastes of star anise and cinnamon. Built at the table with herbs, lime and chilli, every bowl is yours to tune.

method.title: char the aromatics
method.body: Char the onion and ginger over a flame or in a dry pan until blackened, for a smoky depth.

method.title: toast and simmer
method.body: Toast the whole spices, then simmer with the stock, aromatics and fish sauce. Strain.

method.title: noodles
method.body: Soak or cook the rice noodles, then divide between bowls.

method.title: assemble
method.body: Lay the raw sliced beef over the noodles, it cooks in the broth.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pour the boiling broth over and serve with herbs, beansprouts, lime and chilli.

title: Broccoli Cheddar Soup
story: Comfort by the bowlful. A creamy, cheesy soup with enough broccoli to let you call it healthy. A roux gives it body, a mature cheddar gives it punch. Blend it smooth or leave it chunky, either way the cheese toast is mandatory.

method.title: make a roux
method.body: Soften the onion in the butter, stir in the flour and cook out for a minute.

method.title: build
method.body: Gradually whisk in the stock and milk until smooth.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the broccoli and simmer until tender.

method.title: cheese
method.body: Blend to your liking, then stir in the cheddar off the heat until molten.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season well and serve with extra cheese and crusty bread.

title: Lentil Soup
story: Humble, hearty and quietly brilliant. Red lentils melt into a thick, warming soup spiced with cumin and brightened with lemon. Cheap, filling and entirely plant-based, it's the kind of pot that feeds you well all week.

method.title: soften
method.body: Sweat the onion, carrot and garlic in oil until softened.

method.title: spice
method.body: Stir in the cumin and coriander until fragrant.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the lentils and stock and simmer until the lentils collapse and thicken.

method.title: finish
method.body: Blend a little if you like it smooth, then sharpen with lemon and season well.

title: Clam Chowder
story: New England in a bowl: thick, creamy and studded with clams and potato. Bacon gives it a smoky backbone, cream the richness. Don't let it boil once the dairy's in. Serve with crackers, or in a hollowed sourdough if you're going all in.

method.title: crisp the bacon
method.body: Fry the bacon until crisp, then soften the onion in the fat.

method.title: thicken
method.body: Stir in the flour and cook out, then gradually whisk in the stock.

method.title: simmer the potato
method.body: Add the potatoes and simmer until tender.

method.title: finish gently
method.body: Stir in the cream and clams and warm through without boiling.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season, scatter with parsley and serve with crackers or sourdough.

title: Pea and Ham Soup
story: A frugal classic with serious depth. A ham hock simmered low gives a smoky, savoury stock that the split peas drink up as they melt into a thick, comforting soup. The shredded ham stirred back in at the end is the reward for the wait.

method.title: simmer the hock
method.body: Cover the ham hock with water, add the bay and simmer low until the meat is falling off the bone.

method.title: add the peas
method.body: Lift out the hock, then add the split peas and vegetables to the smoky stock.

method.title: cook down
method.body: Simmer until the peas collapse into a thick soup, stirring now and then.

method.title: shred and return
method.body: Shred the ham off the bone and stir it back in. Season, the stock may need little salt.

method.title: serve
method.body: Ladle into bowls with plenty of bread and butter.

title: Beef Stew
story: Low, slow and deeply restorative. Browned beef and root vegetables braised in a rich, savoury gravy until the meat shreds at the touch of a spoon. It asks for almost nothing but time, and pays it all back. Dumplings optional but encouraged.

method.title: brown the beef
method.body: Toss the beef in seasoned flour and brown hard in batches. Colour is the foundation of the gravy.

method.title: soften the veg
method.body: Soften the onion, carrot and celery, then stir in the tomato purée.

method.title: deglaze
method.body: Pour in a little stock and scrape up all the sticky bits from the pan.

method.title: braise low
method.body: Return the beef, add the rest of the stock, cover and cook low until the meat is meltingly tender.

method.title: finish
method.body: Season the rich gravy and serve with mash or crusty bread.

title: Beef Bourguignon
story: The dish that makes a kitchen smell like a bistro. Beef braised slowly in red wine with smoky lardons, button mushrooms and baby onions until impossibly tender. A little effort, a long simmer, and an unforgettable result. Worth a bottle of decent wine.

method.title: render the lardons
method.body: Crisp the lardons, then set aside, leaving the fat in the pan.

method.title: brown the beef
method.body: Toss the beef in flour and brown hard in batches in the bacon fat.

method.title: deglaze
method.body: Stir in the tomato purée, pour in the wine and reduce a little, scraping the pan.

method.title: braise
method.body: Add the stock and lardons, cover and cook low until the beef is fork-tender.

method.title: garnish
method.body: Fry the mushrooms and onions until golden, stir in and simmer to finish.

title: Chili con Carne
story: A big, generous pot of spiced beef and beans that only gets better the next day. Cumin, smoked paprika and a little dark chocolate build deep, smoky warmth. Pile it on rice, into wraps or over nachos. Feeds a crowd, barely any work.

method.title: brown the beef
method.body: Brown the mince hard, breaking it up, then add the onion and pepper to soften.

method.title: spice it
method.body: Stir in the spice mix and cook until fragrant.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the tomatoes, beans and a splash of water and simmer low until thick and rich.

method.title: deepen
method.body: Stir in the dark chocolate for a smoky depth, then season well.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pile over rice with all the toppings: soured cream, cheese, lime.

title: White Chicken Chili
story: A creamy, gentle twist on chilli that went everywhere for good reason. Chicken and white beans in a mild green-chilli broth, finished with a little cream and lime. Cosy, comforting and quietly addictive. The toppings make it a feast.

method.title: brown the chicken
method.body: Brown the chicken with the onion and green chillies until starting to colour.

method.title: spice
method.body: Stir in the spice mix until fragrant.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the beans and stock and simmer until the chicken is tender and the broth thickens. Mash some beans to thicken.

method.title: finish
method.body: Stir in the soured cream and a squeeze of lime off the heat.

method.title: serve
method.body: Top with coriander, avocado, cheese and tortilla chips.

title: Irish Stew
story: Honest, gentle and rooted in not much at all. Lamb, potato and onion layered and simmered slowly until the potatoes thicken the broth and the lamb falls apart. No browning, no fuss, just patience and good ingredients. Comfort defined.

method.title: layer
method.body: Layer the lamb, onion, carrot and most of the potato in a heavy pot, seasoning as you go.

method.title: add stock
method.body: Pour in the stock to almost cover and tuck in the thyme.

method.title: simmer low
method.body: Cover and cook gently until the lamb is tender and the broth has body.

method.title: thicken
method.body: Add the rest of the potato and cook until it breaks down a little to thicken the stew.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season, scatter with parsley and serve with soda bread.

title: Hungarian Goulash
story: More than a stew, almost a soup. Beef simmered slowly with a serious amount of sweet paprika and peppers until the broth turns deep red and rich. Caraway and a little vinegar keep it lively. Spoon it over noodles or just mop with bread.

method.title: soften the onion
method.body: Cook the onions low and slow until soft and golden.

method.title: bloom the paprika
method.body: Off the heat, stir in the paprika and caraway so it doesn't scorch and turn bitter.

method.title: build
method.body: Add the beef, peppers, tomatoes and stock and bring to a simmer.

method.title: braise
method.body: Cover and cook low until the beef is meltingly tender and the broth is rich and red.

method.title: finish
method.body: Sharpen with the vinegar, season and serve with noodles or bread.

title: Beef Stroganoff
story: Quick, creamy and a little bit retro in the best way. Strips of beef seared fast, mushrooms browned hard, all brought together in a soured-cream and mustard sauce. On the table in half an hour, classically over buttered rice or noodles.

method.title: sear the beef
method.body: Sear the beef strips hard and fast in a hot pan, then lift out, it should stay pink.

method.title: brown the mushrooms
method.body: Brown the mushrooms and onion in the same pan until deep golden.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Stir in the paprika, then the soured cream and mustard to a glossy sauce.

method.title: combine
method.body: Return the beef and any juices and warm through gently without boiling.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with parsley and serve over rice or noodles.

title: Gumbo
story: Louisiana in a pot, built on a roux cooked dark as chocolate. Smoky sausage, chicken and the holy trinity of onion, pepper and celery simmer into a deep, soulful stew. Take the roux slow and low, it's the heart of the whole thing.

method.title: make the roux
method.body: Cook the flour and oil together over low heat, stirring constantly, until it turns the colour of dark chocolate. This is the soul of gumbo, don't burn it.

method.title: the trinity
method.body: Stir in the onion, pepper and celery and cook until softened.

method.title: build
method.body: Add the sausage, chicken and cajun seasoning, then gradually whisk in the stock.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Simmer low until rich, thickened and deeply flavoured.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season and serve over rice with spring onion.

title: Caesar Salad
story: The salad that eats like a treat. Crisp cos, garlicky croutons and shavings of parmesan in a creamy, savoury dressing built on anchovy and lemon. Make the dressing properly, that umami punch is what people are really after. Add chicken to make it dinner.

method.title: make croutons
method.body: Toss the bread in oil and bake or fry until golden and crunchy.

method.title: make the dressing
method.body: Mash the anchovy and garlic, whisk in the yolk, lemon and parmesan, then stream in the oil to emulsify.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss the lettuce with the dressing until every leaf is coated.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pile up with the croutons and shaved parmesan, and plenty of black pepper.

title: Greek Salad
story: Sunshine on a plate, and not a lettuce leaf in sight. Ripe tomatoes, cool cucumber, sharp red onion and salty olives, topped with a proper slab of feta and a shower of oregano. The secret is good olive oil and letting it sit so the juices mingle.

method.title: chop
method.body: Chunk the tomatoes and cucumber into generous pieces and thinly slice the onion.

method.title: combine
method.body: Toss the vegetables and olives together in a bowl with a little salt.

method.title: top
method.body: Lay the slab of feta on top, don't crumble it, that's the Greek way.

method.title: dress
method.body: Whisk the oil, vinegar and oregano and pour over. Let it sit five minutes before serving.

title: Cobb Salad
story: A salad that thinks it's a main, and it's right. Rows of chicken, bacon, egg, avocado, tomato and blue cheese over crisp leaves, with a tangy dressing. The joy is in the arrangement and the variety, a little of everything in each forkful.

method.title: boil the eggs
method.body: Hard boil the eggs, cool in cold water, peel and quarter.

method.title: crisp the bacon
method.body: Fry the bacon until crisp, then chop.

method.title: arrange
method.body: Spread the leaves on a platter and lay the chicken, bacon, egg, avocado, tomato and blue cheese in neat rows.

method.title: dress
method.body: Whisk the dressing and drizzle over just before serving.

title: Caprese Salad
story: Three ingredients, total harmony. Ripe tomato, milky mozzarella and fragrant basil, dressed simply with olive oil and salt. There's nowhere to hide, so use the best tomatoes and mozzarella you can find and eat it at room temperature.

method.title: slice
method.body: Slice the tomatoes thick and tear the mozzarella into rough pieces.

method.title: arrange
method.body: Layer the tomato and mozzarella on a plate, tucking basil leaves between.

method.title: dress
method.body: Drizzle generously with olive oil and finish with flaky salt and pepper.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve at room temperature with bread to mop the juices.

title: Pasta Salad
story: The picnic and packed-lunch hero. Cooked pasta tossed with crunchy veg, salty cheese and a punchy dressing that soaks in as it sits. Endlessly adaptable, it actually tastes better made ahead, which makes it the ultimate prepare-and-forget dish.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the pasta until just tender, then cool under cold water so it stays firm.

method.title: prep the veg
method.body: Halve the tomatoes, dice the pepper and slice the onion.

method.title: toss
method.body: Whisk the dressing and toss everything together with plenty of seasoning.

method.title: rest
method.body: Leave for ten minutes, or chill, so the flavours soak in before serving.

title: Potato Salad
story: A barbecue and buffet essential. Waxy potatoes dressed while just warm so they drink up the creamy, mustardy dressing, finished with crunch from spring onion and a fresh hit of herbs. Dressing warm is the trick everyone forgets.

method.title: boil the potatoes
method.body: Boil the potatoes in salted water until just tender, then drain.

method.title: mix the dressing
method.body: Stir the mayo, yoghurt and mustard together with seasoning.

method.title: dress warm
method.body: Toss the warm potatoes in the dressing so they soak it up.

method.title: finish
method.body: Fold through the spring onion and chives. Serve warm or chilled.

title: Coleslaw
story: Crunchy, creamy and miles better than shop-bought. Finely shredded cabbage and carrot in a tangy, lightly sweet dressing. The trick is salting the cabbage briefly to keep it crisp, not watery. The perfect cool foil to anything off the grill.

method.title: shred
method.body: Finely shred the cabbage and onion and grate the carrot.

method.title: salt briefly
method.body: Toss the cabbage with a little salt and leave five minutes, then squeeze out excess water to keep it crunchy.

method.title: dress
method.body: Whisk the dressing and fold through the vegetables.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season and serve, or chill for an hour to let it mellow.

title: Nicoise Salad
story: A composed salad that feels like a holiday. Tuna, green beans, new potatoes, egg, olives and tomato, arranged rather than tossed, with a sharp mustard dressing. Each element seasoned on its own makes the whole plate sing. A proper lunch in the sun.

method.title: boil the potatoes
method.body: Boil the potatoes until tender, adding the beans for the last three minutes. Drain.

method.title: the eggs
method.body: Boil the eggs for eight minutes for just-set yolks, cool and halve.

method.title: sear the tuna
method.body: If using fresh tuna, sear hard for a minute each side so it stays pink. Slice.

method.title: compose
method.body: Arrange everything in groups on a platter, season each part.

method.title: dress
method.body: Whisk the dressing and spoon over just before serving.

title: Waldorf Salad
story: A crisp, refreshing classic from a grand old hotel. Crunchy apple and celery, sweet grapes and toasted walnuts in a light, creamy dressing. The contrast of crunch, sweetness and a little tang is what's kept it on menus for a century.

method.title: toast the walnuts
method.body: Toast the walnuts in a dry pan until fragrant, then roughly chop.

method.title: prep
method.body: Dice the apple and toss with a little lemon to stop it browning, then slice the celery and halve the grapes.

method.title: dress
method.body: Mix the mayo, yoghurt and lemon and fold through the apple, celery, grapes and most of the walnuts.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pile onto lettuce leaves and scatter with the last walnuts.

title: Tabbouleh
story: Herb-forward and bright as anything. Real tabbouleh is mostly parsley and mint, with just a little bulgur for texture, sharpened with lemon and good olive oil. Chop the herbs by hand and don't drown the wheat. Fresh, green and endlessly moreish.

method.title: soak the bulgur
method.body: Cover the bulgur with a little boiling water and leave to soften, then fluff and cool.

method.title: chop the herbs
method.body: Finely chop the parsley and mint by hand, the heart of the dish.

method.title: combine
method.body: Mix the herbs, tomato, spring onion and bulgur together.

method.title: dress
method.body: Dress with lemon, oil and salt and toss. Taste, it should be sharp and fresh.

title: Quinoa Salad
story: The packed-lunch upgrade that actually fills you up. Fluffy quinoa tossed with crunchy veg, herbs and a zingy lemon dressing, with feta or chickpeas for substance. Cook the quinoa properly and dry it out a little so it stays light, not claggy.

method.title: cook the quinoa
method.body: Simmer the quinoa until the grains uncurl, then drain and spread out to steam dry and cool.

method.title: prep
method.body: Dice the cucumber, halve the tomatoes and chop the herbs.

method.title: toss
method.body: Combine the quinoa, vegetables and chickpeas with the lemon, oil and seasoning.

method.title: finish
method.body: Fold through the feta and herbs and serve.

title: Panzanella
story: The Tuscan answer to stale bread, and a riot of summer flavour. Crusty bread soaks up the juices of the ripest tomatoes, sharpened with vinegar and scattered with basil. Let it sit so the bread half-softens, that mingling is the whole magic.

method.title: crisp the bread
method.body: Tear the bread and toast or bake until crisp and golden at the edges.

method.title: salt the tomatoes
method.body: Chunk the tomatoes, toss with salt and leave so they release their juices.

method.title: combine
method.body: Mix the bread, tomatoes and their juice, onion and cucumber with the oil and vinegar.

method.title: rest
method.body: Leave ten minutes so the bread soaks up the juices, then tear in basil and serve.

title: Ratatouille
story: Provence in a pot, all sun-ripened summer veg. Aubergine, courgette and peppers stewed slowly with tomato until soft, glossy and deeply savoury. Cooking each vegetable a little separately keeps them distinct rather than mushy. Rustic, generous and even better the next day.

method.title: brown the aubergine
method.body: Fry the aubergine in oil until golden and soft, then set aside, it soaks up oil so do it alone.

method.title: the rest
method.body: Soften the onion, peppers and courgette until starting to colour, then add the garlic.

method.title: build
method.body: Return the aubergine, add the tomatoes and herbs and bring to a simmer.

method.title: stew
method.body: Simmer low until everything is soft, glossy and rich. Season well.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with basil and a drizzle of oil. Great hot or at room temperature.

title: Stuffed Peppers
story: Edible bowls of comfort. Sweet peppers roasted until soft and filled with a savoury mix of rice, vegetables and cheese. A complete meal in a single colourful package, and a brilliant way to stretch leftovers. The peppers go jammy and sweet as they roast.

method.title: prep the peppers
method.body: Halve the peppers and scoop out the seeds, then sit them in a baking dish.

method.title: make the filling
method.body: Soften the onion, stir in the paprika, tomatoes and rice and season well.

method.title: stuff
method.body: Pile the filling into the peppers and top with cheese.

method.title: roast
method.body: Roast at 190C fan until the peppers are soft and the cheese is golden.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with herbs and serve with a green salad.

title: Vegetable Stir Fry
story: Dinner in the time it takes to cook rice. Crisp, colourful veg tossed fast over a fierce heat with a glossy soy and ginger sauce, so everything stays bright and crunchy. The secret is high heat, prepped ingredients and not crowding the pan. Endlessly adaptable.

method.title: prep everything
method.body: Slice all the veg before you start, stir-frying moves too fast to chop as you go.

method.title: hard veg first
method.body: Get the wok smoking, add a little oil and stir-fry the broccoli and carrot first.

method.title: the rest
method.body: Add the pepper, mangetout, ginger and garlic and toss over high heat.

method.title: sauce
method.body: Pour in the sauce and toss until everything is glossy and just tender.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with sesame seeds and serve over rice or noodles.

title: Cauliflower Cheese
story: The roast dinner's best supporting act, often the bit people fight over. Tender cauliflower under a glossy, mustard-spiked cheese sauce, baked until bubbling and golden. A good sharp cheddar and a confident hand with the seasoning make all the difference.

method.title: blanch
method.body: Boil the cauliflower until just tender, then drain really well.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, then gradually whisk in the milk to a smooth sauce.

method.title: cheese it
method.body: Off the heat, stir in most of the cheese and the mustard. Season well.

method.title: assemble
method.body: Sit the cauliflower in a dish, pour over the sauce and top with the rest of the cheese.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 200C fan until bubbling and golden on top.

title: Roasted Vegetables
story: The easiest way to make vegetables irresistible. A tumble of seasonal veg, oil, salt and high heat, roasted until the edges caramelise and the insides go sweet and soft. Almost no effort, endlessly flexible, and good hot, cold, in salads or alongside anything.

method.title: chop
method.body: Cut everything into similar-sized chunks so they roast evenly.

method.title: toss
method.body: Toss with the oil, garlic and plenty of salt and pepper on a big tray.

method.title: spread out
method.body: Spread in a single layer with space, crowding makes them steam instead of caramelise.

method.title: roast
method.body: Roast at 200C fan, turning once, until tender and caramelised at the edges.

method.title: finish
method.body: Finish with herbs, a squeeze of lemon or a drizzle of balsamic.

title: Vegetable Chili
story: All the smoky, spiced comfort of a chilli, no meat needed. Beans and vegetables simmered with cumin, paprika and chipotle into a deep, hearty pot that even committed carnivores go back to. A little dark chocolate at the end gives it a rich, grown-up depth.

method.title: soften
method.body: Soften the onion and peppers until sweet.

method.title: spice
method.body: Stir in the spice mix and cook until fragrant.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the tomatoes, beans and corn and simmer until thick and rich. Mash some beans to thicken.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season, then pile over rice with avocado, soured cream and lime.

title: Tofu Stir Fry
story: Crispy tofu that even doubters come round to. Pressing and frying the tofu hard gives golden, chewy edges that soak up a sticky soy-and-garlic glaze. Toss with crunchy veg over high heat and it's a fast, satisfying plant-based dinner with real texture.

method.title: press the tofu
method.body: Press the tofu to squeeze out water, then cube it, the key to crispness.

method.title: crisp
method.body: Toss the tofu in cornflour and fry until golden and crisp all over, then set aside.

method.title: veg
method.body: Stir-fry the broccoli, pepper and garlic over high heat until just tender.

method.title: glaze
method.body: Return the tofu, pour in the sauce and toss until sticky and glossy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve over rice with sesame seeds and spring onion.

title: Stuffed Mushrooms
story: Little savoury bites that work as a starter or a side. Big mushrooms filled with a garlicky, herby breadcrumb mix and baked until the tops are crisp and golden and the mushrooms juicy beneath. Quick to throw together and they vanish off the plate.

method.title: prep
method.body: Remove the mushroom stalks, chop them finely and sit the caps on a tray.

method.title: make the filling
method.body: Mix the chopped stalks with the breadcrumbs, garlic, parsley, parmesan and oil.

method.title: stuff
method.body: Pile the filling into each cap and press down lightly.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 190C fan until the mushrooms are soft and the tops crisp and golden.

title: Falafel
story: Crisp, herby and green at the heart. Made from soaked dried chickpeas, never tinned, blitzed raw with herbs and spices then fried until shatteringly crisp. The dried-chickpea rule is what keeps them light, not dense. Stuff into pita with everything.

method.title: blitz
method.body: Blitz the soaked raw chickpeas with the onion, garlic, herbs and spices to a coarse, sandy paste.

method.title: rest
method.body: Stir in the baking powder and chill so the mix firms up and holds together.

method.title: shape
method.body: Roll into small balls or patties.

method.title: fry
method.body: Deep or shallow fry until deep golden and crisp, then drain.

method.title: serve
method.body: Stuff into warm pita with salad, pickles and tahini sauce.

title: Halloumi Traybake
story: One tray, hardly any washing up, maximum flavour. Mediterranean veg and chickpeas roasted until caramelised, with slabs of halloumi added near the end so they go golden and squeaky. A complete, colourful dinner that practically cooks itself.

method.title: prep
method.body: Chop the veg into chunks and tip onto a big tray with the chickpeas.

method.title: season
method.body: Toss with oil, paprika and seasoning and spread out.

method.title: roast
method.body: Roast at 200C fan until the veg is tender and starting to caramelise.

method.title: add halloumi
method.body: Lay the halloumi slices on top and roast until golden and soft.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with lemon, herbs and a drizzle of oil.

title: Veggie Burger
story: A patty with enough bite to stand on its own. Black beans and mushrooms give it a meaty texture, breadcrumbs and a good chill keep it from falling apart. Crisp on the outside, savoury within, and built for all the proper burger trimmings.

method.title: cook the mushrooms
method.body: Fry the mushrooms and onion until soft and any water has cooked off, so the mix isn't wet.

method.title: mash
method.body: Mash the beans coarsely, then mix with the mushrooms, breadcrumbs and paprika. Season.

method.title: shape and chill
method.body: Form into patties and chill so they firm up and hold together.

method.title: fry
method.body: Fry in a little oil until crisp and golden on both sides.

method.title: build
method.body: Stack in toasted buns with all the trimmings and sauce.

title: Aubergine Parmigiana
story: Layered, rich and deeply comforting. Slices of aubergine baked under tomato sauce and molten mozzarella and parmesan until bubbling and golden. Salting and roasting the aubergine first keeps it silky, not bitter or greasy. A meat-free centrepiece that satisfies completely.

method.title: roast the aubergine
method.body: Brush the slices with oil and roast until golden and soft, this keeps them silky.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Simmer the passata with the garlic and basil into a thick, rich tomato sauce.

method.title: layer
method.body: Layer aubergine, sauce, mozzarella and parmesan in a dish, repeating to the top.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 190C fan until bubbling and golden.

method.title: rest
method.body: Let it settle ten minutes so it slices cleanly. Serve with bread and salad.

title: Spanakopita
story: Crisp, golden filo wrapped around a savoury greens-and-feta filling. The contrast of shatteringly crisp pastry and soft, herby spinach within is the whole joy. Squeezing the spinach really dry and brushing each layer with butter is what gives the crunch.

method.title: wilt and dry
method.body: Wilt the spinach, then squeeze it really dry, any water makes the pastry soggy.

method.title: mix the filling
method.body: Mix the spinach with feta, spring onion, herbs and beaten egg. Season.

method.title: layer the filo
method.body: Brush sheets of filo with butter and layer half into a tin, letting them overhang.

method.title: fill and top
method.body: Spread the filling, then layer the remaining buttered filo on top and tuck in.

method.title: bake
method.body: Score the top and bake at 180C fan until deep golden and crisp.

title: Bean Burrito
story: A handheld dinner that's cheap, filling and properly satisfying. Spiced beans, rice and cheese wrapped tight and griddled until the tortilla is crisp and the inside molten. Meat-free but in no way lacking. Build a batch and they freeze brilliantly.

method.title: spice the beans
method.body: Soften the onion, stir in the spices, then add the beans and a splash of water and mash to a rough paste.

method.title: warm the tortillas
method.body: Warm the tortillas so they roll without splitting.

method.title: build
method.body: Pile beans, rice, cheese and salsa down the middle of each.

method.title: wrap
method.body: Fold in the sides and roll up tight.

method.title: griddle
method.body: Griddle seam-down until crisp and golden and the cheese melts.

title: Fluffy Pancakes
story: Tall, cloud-soft and golden. The American-style stack, leavened with baking powder so they puff up thick in the pan. The batter wants to be lumpy and rested, not smooth and beaten. Stack them high, drown in maple syrup and don't overthink it.

method.title: mix dry
method.body: Whisk the flour, baking powder, sugar and a pinch of salt together.

method.title: combine
method.body: Whisk in the egg, milk and melted butter to a thick, lumpy batter. Don't overmix.

method.title: rest
method.body: Let the batter sit so the baking powder gets to work, this gives you the fluff.

method.title: cook
method.body: Drop ladles into a hot buttered pan, flip when bubbles burst on top, cook till golden.

method.title: stack
method.body: Pile them up with butter, maple syrup and berries.

title: French Toast
story: Stale bread's finest hour. Thick slices soaked in sweet, vanilla-spiced custard, then fried in butter until caramelised and crisp outside, soft and rich within. Use bread a day or two old so it drinks up the custard without falling apart.

method.title: make the custard
method.body: Whisk the eggs, milk, vanilla and cinnamon together in a shallow dish.

method.title: soak
method.body: Dip each slice in the custard, letting it soak through but not collapse.

method.title: fry
method.body: Fry in foaming butter over medium heat until golden and caramelised on both sides.

method.title: serve
method.body: Dust with icing sugar and serve with syrup and fruit.

title: Scrambled Eggs
story: The simplest cook, the easiest to ruin. Soft, glossy curds come from low heat, gentle stirring and pulling the pan early, because they keep cooking off the heat. Butter generously, season at the end, and serve on hot buttered toast. That's it.

method.title: beat
method.body: Beat the eggs lightly with a pinch of salt, just until the yolks break.

method.title: low and slow
method.body: Melt the butter over low heat, add the eggs and stir gently and constantly.

method.title: pull early
method.body: When still glossy and a touch underdone, take the pan off, they finish in the residual heat.

method.title: serve
method.body: Season and tip straight onto hot buttered toast.

title: Omelette
story: A two-minute meal that tests your nerve. Beaten eggs cooked fast in a hot, buttery pan, agitated then left to set just enough to fold around a filling. The French way keeps it pale and barely set; rustic is fine too. Speed and a good pan are everything.

method.title: beat
method.body: Beat the eggs well with a little salt until fully combined.

method.title: hot pan
method.body: Melt the butter in a non-stick pan until foaming, then pour in the eggs.

method.title: agitate
method.body: Stir and shake for a few seconds, then let the base set while the top stays a little soft.

method.title: fill and fold
method.body: Scatter cheese and chives over one half, then fold and slide onto a plate.

title: Overnight Oats
story: Breakfast that makes itself while you sleep. Oats soaked overnight in milk and yoghurt go soft, creamy and ready to grab. Layer with fruit and a swirl of nut butter and you've a proper breakfast with zero morning effort. Endlessly customisable.

method.title: mix
method.body: Stir the oats, milk, yoghurt and honey together in a jar or bowl.

method.title: chill
method.body: Cover and refrigerate overnight so the oats soften and thicken.

method.title: loosen
method.body: In the morning, stir and add a splash more milk if it's too thick.

method.title: top
method.body: Layer with fresh fruit, nut butter and a few seeds.

title: Chia Pudding
story: The little seed that does all the work. Chia swells in milk into a thick, tapioca-like pudding overnight, no cooking at all. Naturally packed with fibre and easy to flavour, it's the make-ahead breakfast that took over every feed for good reason.

method.title: whisk
method.body: Whisk the chia, milk, maple and vanilla together really well so it doesn't clump.

method.title: stir again
method.body: Wait five minutes and whisk once more to break up any clumps, the key to a smooth pudding.

method.title: chill
method.body: Cover and refrigerate overnight until thick and set.

method.title: top
method.body: Stir, loosen if needed, and layer with fruit and granola.

title: Cottage Cheese Bowl
story: The humble tub that became a sensation. High in protein and endlessly versatile, cottage cheese goes savoury or sweet depending on your mood and your morning. A bowl built in two minutes that actually keeps you full. Blend it if the texture's not your thing.

method.title: base
method.body: Spoon the cottage cheese into a bowl. Blend it first for a smooth, whipped texture if you prefer.

method.title: top
method.body: Pile on the fruit, drizzle with honey and scatter with granola and seeds.

method.title: serve
method.body: Eat straight away while the granola is still crunchy.

title: Breakfast Burrito
story: Everything you want from breakfast, wrapped to go. Fluffy eggs, crispy potato, cheese and beans rolled tight in a warm tortilla, with hot sauce never far away. Build it, wrap it, griddle the seam so it crisps and holds. The ultimate weekend handful.

method.title: crisp the potato
method.body: Fry the diced potato until golden and crisp, then season.

method.title: scramble
method.body: Softly scramble the eggs and warm the beans through.

method.title: warm tortillas
method.body: Warm the tortillas so they roll without cracking.

method.title: build
method.body: Pile egg, potato, beans, cheese, avocado and salsa down the middle.

method.title: wrap and griddle
method.body: Fold and roll tight, then griddle seam-down until golden and crisp.

title: Homemade Granola
story: Once you make your own you won't go back. Oats, nuts and seeds tossed in honey and oil, baked low until golden and clustered. You control the sweetness and the crunch, and the house smells incredible. The trick to big clusters is not stirring too much.

method.title: mix
method.body: Toss the oats, nuts and seeds with the honey, oil and a pinch of salt until coated.

method.title: spread
method.body: Press into an even layer on a lined tray, packing it down for clusters.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 150C fan, stirring just once halfway, until evenly golden.

method.title: cool and add fruit
method.body: Let it cool fully on the tray to crisp up, then stir through the dried fruit.

title: Belgian Waffles
story: Deep-pocketed, crisp-shelled and impossibly fluffy. The deep grid traps butter and syrup in every square. A little whisked egg white folded through is the secret to that light, airy interior under the crisp golden crust. Worth dragging out the waffle iron.

method.title: mix the batter
method.body: Whisk the flour, baking powder, sugar, egg yolks, milk and melted butter to a smooth batter.

method.title: whip the whites
method.body: Whisk the egg whites to soft peaks, then fold through for lightness.

method.title: heat the iron
method.body: Heat and grease the waffle iron well.

method.title: cook
method.body: Cook in batches until deep golden and crisp, resisting the urge to open early.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve straight away with butter, syrup and fruit.

title: Smoothie Bowl
story: A smoothie thick enough to eat with a spoon. Frozen fruit blended to a soft-serve consistency, then loaded with toppings for crunch and colour. The trick is using frozen fruit and barely any liquid, so it stays thick enough to hold its toppings.

method.title: blend thick
method.body: Blend the frozen fruit with just enough milk and yoghurt to get a thick, scoopable purée. Add liquid sparingly.

method.title: bowl it
method.body: Spoon into bowls and smooth the top.

method.title: top
method.body: Arrange granola, fresh fruit and seeds in neat sections.

method.title: serve
method.body: Eat straight away before it melts.

title: Banana Pancakes
story: Ripe bananas turned into breakfast gold. Mashed straight into the batter, they add natural sweetness and a tender, almost custardy crumb. The blacker the banana the better. A weekend favourite that uses up the fruit bowl and pleases everyone at the table.

method.title: mash
method.body: Mash the bananas well, leaving a few small lumps for texture.

method.title: mix
method.body: Whisk in the flour, baking powder, egg and milk to a thick batter.

method.title: rest
method.body: Let it sit briefly so the baking powder activates.

method.title: cook
method.body: Cook ladles in a hot buttered pan, flipping when bubbles appear and the edges set.

method.title: serve
method.body: Stack with sliced banana, syrup and a little peanut butter.

title: Eggs Benedict
story: The brunch order to beat. A poached egg and ham on a toasted muffin, smothered in warm, buttery hollandaise. It looks like a restaurant trick but it's all about timing, and a hollandaise that comes together with patience and a steady whisk. A proper treat.

method.title: hollandaise
method.body: Whisk the yolks with a splash of water over a pan of simmering water, then slowly stream in the melted butter until thick. Sharpen with lemon, season.

method.title: poach
method.body: Swirl simmering vinegared water and slide in the eggs, poaching until the whites set and yolks stay runny.

method.title: toast
method.body: Toast the muffins and warm the ham.

method.title: build
method.body: Top each muffin half with ham and a drained poached egg.

method.title: finish
method.body: Spoon over the hollandaise and finish with chives and pepper.

title: Shakshuka
story: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato sea, served straight from the pan. Sweet peppers and onions simmer with cumin and paprika into a rich sauce, then eggs are nestled in to set with runny yolks. Scoop it up with warm bread, the only cutlery you need.

method.title: soften
method.body: Soften the onion and pepper in oil until sweet, then add the garlic.

method.title: spice
method.body: Stir in the cumin, paprika and chilli until fragrant.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Add the tomatoes and simmer until thick and rich. Season.

method.title: poach the eggs
method.body: Make wells and crack in the eggs, cover and cook until the whites set but yolks stay soft.

method.title: serve
method.body: Scatter with herbs and feta and serve from the pan with bread.

title: Avocado Toast
story: The brunch staple that launched a thousand cafes. Ripe avocado crushed on properly good toasted bread, with lemon, salt and chilli to lift it. It lives or dies on the details: a ripe avocado, real sourdough and seasoning that's braver than you think.

method.title: toast
method.body: Toast the sourdough until deeply golden and crisp.

method.title: crush
method.body: Crush the avocado with lemon, salt and pepper, leaving it a little chunky.

method.title: top egg
method.body: If using, poach or fry the eggs while the toast is warm.

method.title: build
method.body: Pile the avocado on the toast, add the egg and finish with chilli flakes and oil.

title: Full English Breakfast
story: The weekend institution, plated all at once. Bacon, sausage, eggs, beans, tomato, mushroom and toast, with timing the only real skill. Get everything ready together and hot at once. There's no wrong combination, only the eternal question of where the beans go.

method.title: start the sausages
method.body: Cook the sausages low and slow so they're done through, turning for even colour.

method.title: bacon and veg
method.body: Add the bacon, tomatoes and mushrooms and cook until everything is browned.

method.title: beans and toast
method.body: Warm the beans and toast the bread while the rest finishes.

method.title: fry the eggs
method.body: Fry the eggs last so the yolks stay runny and hot.

method.title: plate
method.body: Plate everything together and serve at once with brown sauce or ketchup.

title: Huevos Rancheros
story: A farmhand's breakfast with serious punch. Fried eggs over warm tortillas, smothered in a smoky, chilli-spiked tomato salsa, with beans and avocado alongside. Built to set you up for a hard day, it's bold, brilliant and best eaten with a fork in each hand.

method.title: make the salsa
method.body: Soften the onion, garlic and chilli, stir in the spices, then add the tomatoes and simmer to a thick salsa.

method.title: warm beans and tortillas
method.body: Warm the beans and char the tortillas lightly over a flame or in a dry pan.

method.title: fry the eggs
method.body: Fry the eggs so the whites are crisp at the edges and yolks runny.

method.title: build
method.body: Lay tortillas on plates, add beans, then the eggs.

method.title: serve
method.body: Spoon over the salsa and finish with avocado, coriander and lime.

title: Breakfast Hash
story: A one-pan clear-out that tastes like more than the sum of its parts. Crispy potatoes, onions and whatever else needs using, all fried hard until golden, then topped with a runny egg. The key is patience: leave the potatoes alone so they crisp instead of steam.

method.title: parboil
method.body: Parboil the diced potato for five minutes so it crisps faster in the pan, then drain well.

method.title: crisp the meat
method.body: Fry the chorizo or bacon until its fat renders, then set aside.

method.title: fry the hash
method.body: Fry the potato, onion and pepper in the fat, pressing down and leaving it to crisp before turning.

method.title: combine
method.body: Return the meat, make wells and crack in the eggs.

method.title: finish
method.body: Cover and cook until the eggs set, then scatter with herbs and hot sauce.

title: Fish and Chips
story: The Friday night national treasure. A crisp, golden beer batter shatters to reveal steaming flaky white fish, with proper chunky chips alongside. The trick is ice-cold batter and hot oil for that lacy, crunchy shell. Salt, vinegar and mushy peas non-negotiable.

method.title: first fry the chips
method.body: Fry the chips at a moderate heat until soft but pale, then lift out to drain.

method.title: make the batter
method.body: Whisk the flour, baking powder and cold beer to a thick, smooth batter just before frying.

method.title: batter and fry
method.body: Dip the floured fish in batter and fry in hot oil until deep golden and crisp.

method.title: second fry the chips
method.body: Crank the heat and fry the chips again until crunchy and golden.

method.title: serve
method.body: Drain, salt heavily and serve with mushy peas, tartare and vinegar.

title: Teriyaki Salmon
story: Sweet, sticky and on the table in fifteen minutes. Salmon fillets glazed in a glossy soy, mirin and ginger sauce that caramelises into a lacquer under the heat. Fast enough for a weeknight, smart enough for guests. Serve over rice with the sauce spooned over.

method.title: make the sauce
method.body: Mix the soy, mirin, honey, ginger and garlic together.

method.title: sear
method.body: Sear the salmon skin-side down until crisp, then flip and cook briefly.

method.title: glaze
method.body: Pour in the sauce and let it bubble and reduce, spooning over until thick and glossy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve over rice with the sauce, spring onion and sesame seeds.

title: Garlic Butter Shrimp
story: Plump prawns swimming in a golden pool of garlic butter. It comes together in minutes, so have your bread ready for mopping. A splash of lemon and a flurry of parsley cut the richness. Simple, fast and a little bit indulgent. Don't overcook the prawns.

method.title: melt the butter
method.body: Melt the butter with the garlic and chilli over medium heat until fragrant, not browned.

method.title: cook the prawns
method.body: Add the prawns and cook just until pink and curled, turning once. Don't overcook.

method.title: finish
method.body: Squeeze in the lemon and stir through the parsley. Season.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve with crusty bread to soak up every drop of garlic butter.

title: Fish Tacos
story: Sunshine in a tortilla. Crisp, lightly spiced fish, cool crunchy slaw and a zingy lime crema piled into warm tortillas. The contrast of hot fish and cold, sharp toppings is the whole point. Light, fresh and built for eating with your hands.

method.title: spice the fish
method.body: Toss the fish with the paprika, cumin, salt and a little oil.

method.title: make the slaw
method.body: Toss the cabbage with lime juice and salt for a quick, sharp slaw.

method.title: cook
method.body: Fry the fish hard until cooked through and lightly charred, then flake.

method.title: warm tortillas
method.body: Char the tortillas lightly over a flame or in a dry pan.

method.title: build
method.body: Fill with fish, slaw, lime crema and coriander.

title: Shrimp Scampi
story: Garlic, butter, wine and prawns over pasta, ready before you've laid the table. The sauce is barely a sauce, just emulsified butter, white wine and lemon clinging to the strands. Quick, bright and a little bit fancy. Pasta water is your secret weapon here.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the linguine until al dente, saving a mugful of pasta water.

method.title: garlic butter
method.body: Soften the garlic in butter with a pinch of chilli, not letting it colour.

method.title: prawns and wine
method.body: Add the prawns and wine and simmer until the prawns are pink and the wine reduces.

method.title: emulsify
method.body: Toss in the pasta with a splash of its water and the lemon until glossy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with parsley and plenty of black pepper.

title: Baked Salmon
story: The reliable, no-fuss centrepiece. Salmon roasted with lemon and herbs until the flakes just give and the centre stays moist. Almost impossible to get wrong if you pull it a touch early. Smart enough for company, easy enough for a Tuesday.

method.title: season
method.body: Sit the salmon on a lined tray, drizzle with oil and scatter with garlic, dill and salt.

method.title: top with lemon
method.body: Lay lemon slices over the top.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 180C fan until the salmon flakes easily but stays moist in the middle.

method.title: rest and serve
method.body: Rest briefly, then serve with greens and a squeeze of lemon.

title: Seafood Paella
story: A pan that brings the table together. Saffron-stained rice studded with prawns, mussels and squid, all cooked in one wide pan. The prize is the socarrat, the crisp golden crust on the bottom. Resist stirring once the stock is in and let it form.

method.title: soffritto
method.body: Soften the onion, pepper and garlic, then stir in the paprika and saffron.

method.title: toast the rice
method.body: Stir in the rice to coat it in the flavoured oil.

method.title: add stock
method.body: Pour in the hot stock, spread the rice flat and simmer without stirring.

method.title: add seafood
method.body: Nestle in the prawns, mussels and squid and cook until the shells open and rice is tender.

method.title: socarrat
method.body: Turn up the heat for the last few minutes to crisp the base, then rest and serve with lemon.

title: Crab Cakes
story: All about the crab, barely held together. The best crab cakes use just enough binder to stay whole and let sweet, delicate crab meat shine. Chilled then fried until golden, they're crisp outside and tender within. A squeeze of lemon and you're done.

method.title: mix gently
method.body: Fold the crab with the breadcrumbs, mayo, egg, mustard and spring onion, keeping the lumps of crab intact.

method.title: shape and chill
method.body: Form into patties and chill so they firm up and hold together when fried.

method.title: fry
method.body: Fry in a little oil until deep golden and crisp on both sides.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve with lemon wedges and a tartare or chilli mayo.

title: Tuna Pasta Bake
story: The store-cupboard hero that feeds the whole family. Pasta, tuna and sweetcorn in a creamy cheese sauce, baked under a golden, crunchy top. Cheap, comforting and endlessly reassuring. The kind of midweek dinner everyone secretly loves.

method.title: cook the pasta
method.body: Boil the pasta until just under al dente, since it bakes further, then drain.

method.title: cheese sauce
method.body: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, whisk in the milk and stir in most of the cheese until smooth.

method.title: combine
method.body: Fold the pasta, tuna and sweetcorn through the sauce and season well.

method.title: top
method.body: Tip into a dish and scatter with the remaining cheese.

method.title: bake
method.body: Bake at 200C fan until golden and bubbling.

title: Miso Salmon
story: A glaze that does all the heavy lifting. Sweet-savoury miso caramelises on the salmon under the grill into a deeply umami, sticky lacquer. Just a handful of pantry ingredients for a result that tastes like a restaurant. A little marinating time pays off.

method.title: make the glaze
method.body: Mix the miso, mirin, honey and soy to a smooth paste.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Coat the salmon in the glaze and leave to marinate so the flavour sinks in.

method.title: grill
method.body: Grill under a high heat until the glaze caramelises and the salmon just flakes, watching it doesn't catch.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve over rice with greens, spring onion and sesame.

title: Prawn Stir Fry
story: Fast, fresh and full of snap. Juicy prawns and crisp vegetables tossed over a fierce heat in a savoury, gingery sauce. The whole thing cooks in minutes, so have everything chopped and ready. A weeknight winner that feels far more effort than it is.

method.title: prep
method.body: Slice all the veg and mix the sauce before you start, this moves fast.

method.title: sear the prawns
method.body: Stir-fry the prawns in a screaming-hot wok until just pink, then lift out.

method.title: veg
method.body: Stir-fry the pepper, mangetout, ginger and garlic until crisp-tender.

method.title: combine
method.body: Return the prawns, pour in the sauce and toss until glossy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve over rice or noodles with spring onion.

title: Ceviche
story: Fish cooked without heat, by acid alone. Fresh white fish cured in lime juice turns opaque and firm in minutes, then brightened with chilli, red onion and coriander. It demands the freshest fish you can find. Cool, sharp and utterly refreshing.

method.title: cube the fish
method.body: Cut the fish into even small cubes so it cures evenly.

method.title: cure
method.body: Toss with the lime juice and salt and leave until the fish turns opaque and firm, the lime cooks it.

method.title: combine
method.body: Stir through the onion, chilli and coriander.

method.title: serve
method.body: Serve cold straight away, with tortilla chips or on lettuce.

title: Egg Fried Rice
story: The ultimate use for yesterday's rice. Cold, day-old rice fries up separate and golden where fresh rice turns to mush. A hot wok, a few eggs and a splash of soy is all it takes. Fast, frugal and better than any takeaway when you nail the technique.

method.title: scramble
method.body: Scramble the eggs in a hot, oiled wok, then push to one side or remove.

method.title: fry the rice
method.body: Add the cold rice and toss over high heat, breaking up any clumps, until hot and a little toasty.

method.title: combine
method.body: Stir in the peas, spring onion and egg, then splash in the soy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Finish with sesame oil and more spring onion.

title: Chicken Biryani
story: A celebration in a single pot, layered and steamed. Fragrant spiced chicken and saffron rice are built up in layers and dum-cooked so the flavours mingle and the rice perfumes through. It takes care and a few steps, but lifting the lid is pure theatre.

method.title: marinate
method.body: Marinate the chicken in yoghurt and the spices so it tenderises and soaks up flavour.

method.title: crisp the onions
method.body: Fry the onions until deep golden and crisp, half for the curry, half to layer.

method.title: cook the chicken
method.body: Cook the marinated chicken with the whole spices until rich and almost done.

method.title: parboil the rice
method.body: Boil the basmati until just half-cooked, then drain.

method.title: layer and dum
method.body: Layer rice over chicken, drizzle with saffron milk and crisp onions, cover tightly and steam on low until fluffy.

title: Mushroom Risotto
story: Creamy, comforting and deeply savoury. Arborio rice coaxed slowly with ladles of stock until it turns silky, studded with golden mushrooms and finished with parmesan and butter. The stirring is meditative, the reward is rich. Dried porcini deepen it beautifully.

method.title: brown the mushrooms
method.body: Fry the mushrooms hard until deep golden, then set most aside.

method.title: soften and toast
method.body: Soften the onion, then stir in the rice to toast for a minute.

method.title: deglaze
method.body: Pour in the wine and let it bubble away.

method.title: add stock slowly
method.body: Add hot stock a ladle at a time, stirring, until the rice is creamy and just tender.

method.title: finish
method.body: Stir in the mushrooms, parmesan and a knob of butter. Rest a minute, then serve.

title: Jambalaya
story: A one-pot Louisiana party of rice, smoke and spice. Chicken, sausage and prawns cooked down with the holy trinity and Cajun seasoning, with the rice simmered right in to soak up all the flavour. Hearty, bold and built to feed a hungry crowd.

method.title: brown the meats
method.body: Brown the chicken and sausage to build flavour, then set aside.

method.title: the trinity
method.body: Soften the onion, pepper and celery, then stir in the spices.

method.title: build
method.body: Return the meats, stir in the rice, tomatoes and stock.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Cover and simmer until the rice is almost tender and has soaked up the liquid.

method.title: add prawns
method.body: Stir in the prawns and cook until pink, then rest and serve with spring onion.

title: Nasi Goreng
story: Indonesia's beloved fried rice, sweet, smoky and savoury all at once. Day-old rice fried with kecap manis, a sweet soy that lacquers every grain, topped with a crispy fried egg. Deeply moreish and quick. The runny yolk stirred through is the best part.

method.title: make a paste
method.body: Fry the shallots, garlic and chilli until soft and fragrant.

method.title: fry the rice
method.body: Add the cold rice and toss over high heat until hot and a little toasty.

method.title: sauce
method.body: Pour in the kecap manis and soy and toss until every grain is dark and glossy.

method.title: fry the eggs
method.body: Fry the eggs hard so the whites crisp and the yolks stay runny.

method.title: serve
method.body: Top each plate with a fried egg, crispy shallots and cucumber.

title: Pilau Rice
story: The fragrant rice that turns any curry into a feast. Basmati gently cooked with whole spices, each grain separate, perfumed and golden. The absorption method and a proper rest at the end give you fluffy, never sticky, rice. Whole spices, not ground, are the secret.

method.title: rinse
method.body: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear to wash off excess starch.

method.title: fry the spices
method.body: Soften the onion in oil with the whole spices until golden and fragrant.

method.title: add rice and water
method.body: Stir in the rice to coat, then add the water and a good pinch of salt.

method.title: steam
method.body: Cover and cook on the lowest heat until the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.

method.title: rest and fluff
method.body: Rest off the heat with the lid on, then fluff with a fork.

title: Risotto Milanese
story: The golden risotto of Milan, simple and luxurious. Saffron turns the creamy rice a glowing yellow and lends a subtle, honeyed perfume. Traditionally served with osso buco, but stunning on its own. The bone marrow is classic; butter and parmesan are essential.

method.title: bloom the saffron
method.body: Steep the saffron in a little hot stock to release its colour and aroma.

method.title: soften and toast
method.body: Soften the onion in butter, then stir in the rice to toast briefly.

method.title: deglaze
method.body: Pour in the wine and let it cook away.

method.title: add stock slowly
method.body: Add hot stock a ladle at a time with the saffron, stirring, until creamy and just tender.

method.title: mantecatura
method.body: Beat in cold butter and parmesan off the heat for a glossy finish. Rest, then serve.

title: Kimchi Fried Rice
story: Tangy, spicy and built from the fridge. Fermented kimchi fries down with rice into something deeply savoury and a little funky, crowned with a runny fried egg. A brilliant way to use up older kimchi, whose sourness only makes the dish better. Fast comfort food.

method.title: fry the kimchi
method.body: Fry the chopped kimchi in a little oil until it softens and caramelises.

method.title: add gochujang
method.body: Stir in the gochujang and a splash of the kimchi juice.

method.title: fry the rice
method.body: Add the cold rice and toss over high heat until hot and coated.

method.title: fry the eggs
method.body: Fry the eggs so the yolks stay runny.

method.title: serve
method.body: Top with the egg, spring onion and sesame seeds.

title: Margarita
story: The benchmark by which all cocktails are judged. Tequila, lime and orange liqueur shaken sharp and cold, in a salt-rimmed glass. The balance of sour lime and a whisper of sweetness is everything. Use fresh lime, never a mix, and you'll never look back.

method.title: rim the glass
method.body: Rub a lime wedge round the rim and dip in salt.

method.title: shake
method.body: Shake the tequila, triple sec and lime juice hard with ice until frosty.

method.title: serve
method.body: Strain over fresh ice and garnish with a lime wheel.

title: Mojito
story: Summer in a glass, from Havana. Rum, lime and fresh mint over crushed ice, lengthened with soda for a long, cooling drink. Muddle the mint gently to release the oils without shredding it bitter. Endlessly refreshing on a warm evening.

method.title: muddle
method.body: Gently press the mint with the sugar and lime juice to release the oils, not tear the leaves.

method.title: build
method.body: Add the rum and fill the glass with crushed ice.

method.title: top
method.body: Top with soda, stir gently and garnish with a mint sprig.

title: Pina Colada
story: A holiday you can drink. Rum, pineapple and creamy coconut blended with ice into a tropical, frosty treat. Sweet, smooth and unapologetically fun, it tastes of beaches and not having anywhere to be. Blend until properly slushy for the full effect.

method.title: blend
method.body: Blend the rum, pineapple, coconut cream and ice until thick and slushy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pour into tall glasses and garnish with pineapple and a cherry.

title: Old Fashioned
story: The original cocktail, and still one of the best. Just whiskey, sugar and bitters, stirred over ice and twisted with orange. No shaking, no fuss, all about the spirit. Stir it long and cold to dilute just right. A drink for sipping slowly.

method.title: muddle
method.body: Soak the sugar cube with the bitters and a splash of water and muddle to dissolve.

method.title: stir
method.body: Add the whiskey and a big ice cube and stir long and slow until cold.

method.title: garnish
method.body: Twist the orange peel over the top to release its oils, then drop it in.

title: Espresso Martini
story: The pick-me-up that became a party staple. Vodka, coffee liqueur and a fresh shot of espresso shaken hard until it pours with that signature frothy crema. Cold, sharp and just sweet enough. The fresh espresso, shaken aggressively, is what makes the foam.

method.title: brew
method.body: Pull a fresh shot of espresso and let it cool slightly.

method.title: shake hard
method.body: Shake the vodka, coffee liqueur, espresso and syrup very hard with ice to build the froth.

method.title: serve
method.body: Double strain into a chilled glass and float three coffee beans on the crema.

title: Hot Chocolate
story: Not a powdery sachet, but the real thing. Proper chocolate melted into hot milk until thick, glossy and indulgent. A pinch of salt sharpens it, real chocolate makes it luxurious. The kind of mug you wrap both hands around on a cold day. Add cream if you dare.

method.title: warm the milk
method.body: Heat the milk with the sugar and salt until steaming but not boiling.

method.title: melt the chocolate
method.body: Whisk in the chopped chocolate until smooth, thick and glossy.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pour into mugs and top with cream, marshmallows or a grating of chocolate.

title: Matcha Latte
story: A calm, grassy green pick-me-up. Stone-ground matcha whisked to a smooth froth and topped with steamed milk for a gentler, more sustained lift than coffee. The whisking, traditionally with a bamboo chasen, banishes lumps and builds that signature foam.

method.title: sift and whisk
method.body: Sift the matcha, add the hot water and whisk briskly in a zigzag until smooth and frothy. Boiling water makes it bitter.

method.title: steam the milk
method.body: Warm and froth the milk, sweetening with honey if you like.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pour the milk over the matcha and serve hot, or over ice.

title: Mango Lassi
story: The cooling antidote to a fiery curry. Ripe mango blended with yoghurt into a thick, sweet, tangy drink that soothes the heat and refreshes completely. A pinch of cardamom lifts it. Use the ripest mango you can find for that golden, fragrant sweetness.

method.title: blend
method.body: Blend the mango, yoghurt, milk, honey and cardamom until completely smooth.

method.title: adjust
method.body: Loosen with a little more milk if too thick, and taste for sweetness.

method.title: serve
method.body: Pour over ice and dust with a little more cardamom or pistachio.

title: Marinara Sauce
story: The everyday tomato sauce every cook should know by heart. Just tomatoes, garlic and basil simmered into something rich and bright. It's the backbone of pasta, pizza and so much more. Good tinned tomatoes and a little patience are all it takes. Make a big batch.

method.title: infuse the oil
method.body: Gently warm the garlic in the olive oil until soft and fragrant, not browned.

method.title: add tomatoes
method.body: Tip in the tomatoes with a pinch of sugar and salt.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Simmer gently, stirring now and then, until thickened and rich.

method.title: finish
method.body: Tear in the basil and season. Blend for smooth, or leave rustic.

title: Bechamel Sauce
story: One of the great mother sauces, and simpler than it sounds. A roux of butter and flour cooked out, then milk whisked in to a smooth, velvety white sauce. Master it and lasagne, gratins and cauliflower cheese all open up. Patience and constant whisking beat lumps.

method.title: make a roux
method.body: Melt the butter, stir in the flour and cook out for a couple of minutes without colouring.

method.title: add milk
method.body: Gradually whisk in the warm milk a little at a time, whisking smooth before adding more.

method.title: thicken
method.body: Simmer gently, stirring, until thick enough to coat the back of a spoon.

method.title: season
method.body: Season with salt, pepper and a grating of nutmeg.

title: Basil Pesto
story: Genoa in a jar, and worlds away from shop-bought. Fresh basil, pine nuts, garlic, parmesan and good olive oil pounded into a fragrant green paste. Traditionally made in a pestle and mortar, but a blender works. Toasting the pine nuts deepens the flavour.

method.title: toast the pine nuts
method.body: Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan until golden, watching they don't burn.

method.title: blitz
method.body: Blitz the basil, pine nuts, garlic and parmesan, streaming in the oil until you have a coarse paste.

method.title: season
method.body: Season with salt and a little lemon if you like.

method.title: serve
method.body: Toss through pasta with a splash of pasta water, or spoon over anything.

title: Chimichurri
story: The punchy green sauce that makes grilled meat sing. Finely chopped parsley, garlic, oregano and chilli loosened with red wine vinegar and oil. No cooking, just chopping, and it lifts steak, chicken or veg instantly. Let it sit so the flavours marry before serving.

method.title: chop
method.body: Finely chop the parsley, garlic and chilli by hand for the best texture.

method.title: combine
method.body: Stir in the oregano, vinegar, oil and a good pinch of salt.

method.title: rest
method.body: Leave at room temperature so the flavours mellow and marry.

method.title: serve
method.body: Spoon generously over grilled steak, chicken or vegetables.

title: Hollandaise Sauce
story: The silky, buttery sauce that crowns eggs benedict and asparagus. An emulsion of egg yolk and warm butter, sharpened with lemon. It has a fearsome reputation, but low heat, a steady whisk and patience are all it takes. Whisk hard and don't let it get too hot.

method.title: whisk the yolks
method.body: Whisk the yolks with the vinegar and a splash of water over a pan of barely simmering water until pale and thickened.

method.title: add the butter
method.body: Slowly stream in the warm melted butter, whisking constantly, until thick and glossy. Keep the heat gentle so it doesn't split.

method.title: season
method.body: Sharpen with lemon juice and season with salt.

method.title: serve
method.body: Use straight away, over eggs, fish or asparagus.

title: Hot Honey
story: The sweet-heat condiment that ended up on everything. Honey gently warmed with chilli until it carries a slow, building burn. Drizzle it over pizza, fried chicken, cheese or roasted veg. It takes two minutes to make and transforms whatever it touches.

method.title: warm
method.body: Gently warm the honey with the chilli flakes over low heat, not letting it boil.

method.title: infuse
method.body: Take off the heat and leave to infuse so the heat builds.

method.title: sharpen
method.body: Stir in the vinegar to balance the sweetness.

method.title: store
method.body: Pour into a jar, it keeps for weeks. Strain the flakes if you prefer it smooth.

title: BBQ Sauce
story: Sticky, smoky and miles better than the bottle. A base of tomato and a little molasses simmered with vinegar, spices and smoke until thick and glossy. Sweet, tangy and deeply savoury, it clings to ribs, chicken and burgers. Simmer low so it doesn't catch.

method.title: combine
method.body: Whisk everything together in a pan with a splash of water.

method.title: simmer
method.body: Simmer low and slow, stirring often, until thick and glossy. Watch it doesn't catch.

method.title: adjust
method.body: Taste and balance the sweet, sour and smoke to your liking.

method.title: store
method.body: Cool and keep in a jar, it improves overnight.

title: Roast Gravy
story: The thing that makes a roast a roast. Built on the sticky brown bits and meat juices left in the roasting tin, deglazed with stock and wine into a glossy, savoury gravy. Those caramelised pan drippings are pure flavour, never waste them. The crowning touch on any Sunday.

method.title: make a roux
method.body: Set the roasting tin over a low heat, stir the flour into the fat and juices and cook out.

method.title: deglaze
method.body: Pour in the wine and scrape up every sticky brown bit, that's the flavour.

method.title: add stock
method.body: Gradually whisk in the hot stock and simmer until thickened and glossy.

method.title: strain and serve
method.body: Season, strain if you like it smooth, and pour into a warm jug.
